scored.name: Industry Long-Term Reversal — Sector-SPDR L/S (36-12m)
scored.family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S

scores.name: Industry Long-Term Reversal — Sector-SPDR L/S (36-12m)
scores.oneLineRationale: Clean all-11 re-run (post-2021-07-02) confirms the raw signal is a fragile 2022 value-vs-growth bet (after-cost raw Sharpe collapses 0.30 to 0.04 leave-2022-out, horizon sign-unstable, only ~3 cycles), and the spec's "structurally negative mirror-image" thesis is empirically false and self-refuting (realized OOS ρ to sector_rotation is +0.01/-0.10, near-zero not negative; its own falsifier and daily ρ>0 alert auto-refute in ~51% of windows); BUT the net-of-beta stream is a real, cost-survivable near-zero-ρ slightly-negative-β diversifier (alpha-Sharpe ~0.52 after cost even leave-2022-out, stable β -0.117, favorable selloff tail) on liquid SPDRs — so not CUT, but it cannot ship as-specced: rewrite falsifier/alert to |ρ|<0.35 & |β|<0.25 (not strict negativity), rebrand as a low-correlation defensive ballast, restrict to the clean all-11 window, exclude the 12-1 momentum window from the sign-flip test, and size tiny.

spec.name: Industry Long-Term Reversal — Sector-SPDR L/S (36-12m)
spec.family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S
spec.mechanism: Industry-level long-term return reversal (ScienceDirect 'Industry long-term return reversal'): sectors that have led/lagged over a 3-5y horizon subsequently reverse, with valuation effects persisting for years — harder to dismiss as microcap/illiquidity noise because it operates on broad, deeply liquid indices. Economically it is slow cyclical mean-reversion of sector valuations (a sector-level value/contrarian premium) and is the cleanest, ZERO-survivorship expression of the family: indices cannot be delisted, so the long-loser-sector leg carries no dead-name bias. Being a SLOW contrarian sector tilt it is the mirror-image of the book's sector_rotation (fast 3m sector MOMENTUM) and xs_momentum, giving structurally negative correlation.
spec.exactSignal: Universe = the 11 sector SPDRs (XLK,XLF,XLV,XLE,XLI,XLY,XLP,XLB,XLU,XLC,XLRE). For each ETF: formationRet=close[t-21]/close[t-756]-1 (36-month total return, skip last 21d). De-mean across the 11 sectors: score = -(formationRet - mean(formationRet across the 11)) so the most-lagged-vs-peers sector gets the highest (LONG) score and the strongest gets the most negative (SHORT) score. This demeaning makes it market-neutral by construction (the equal-weighted sector basket is netted out).
spec.entryExit: Long the 4 highest-score (most-lagged) sectors, short the 4 lowest-score (most-led) sectors, equal-weight: each long +0.125, each short -0.125 (gross ~1.0, dollar- and beta-neutral since longs/shorts are both broad equity betas). Rebalance every 21 trading days; the middle 3 sectors are untraded. No stops.
spec.instruments: 11 GICS sector SPDR ETFs only (long + short). No single names — eliminates survivorship bias entirely.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low — trades only sector index ETFs, which are not subject to single-name delisting/survivorship bias at all; the long-loser leg is a live, rebalanced index. The only minor caveat is XLC/XLRE have shorter histories (post-2015/2018), trimming the breadth and OOS window slightly.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: mark the 8-ETF L/S book, log per-sector contribution, rolling 60d Sharpe and gross, and the 60d ρ of daily PnL to sector_rotation (must stay negative — it is the slow contrarian mirror) and to xs_momentum/cross_asset_trend. Alert if 60d ρ to sector_rotation rises above 0 (the diversification thesis breaking) or rolling 6m Sharpe < -0.3.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, if the sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe is <=0.10, OR its OOS daily-PnL correlation to the existing sector_rotation sleeve is NOT negative (i.e. it fails to provide the contrarian sector diversification it claims), OR a 12-1 vs 36-12 horizon sensitivity sweep shows the sign of the edge flips across adjacent windows (curve-fit, not a stable reversal), the sleeve is refuted and dropped.
spec._family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S

scored.name: Turn-of-Month Broad-Equity Flow Premium (calendar overlay, beta-neutralized)
scored.family: Calendar/flow effects with a documented mechanism (FOMC information cycle + turn-of-month liquidity flow), constructed market-neutral and judged ruthlessly on post-2021 OOS net-of-cost Sharpe

scores.name: Turn-of-Month Broad-Equity Flow Premium (calendar overlay, beta-neutralized)
scores.oneLineRationale: Independently reproduced on real 11y SPY OHLCV: genuinely orthogonal (rho -0.04 SPY, -0.03 to momentum book, no crash tail) and cleanly backtestable, but the edge INVERTED OOS (in-minus-out spread -3.31bps/d, Welch-t -0.43; overlay net-of-cost Sharpe -0.23), the spec's own ship-gate fires 0/96 rolling windows (max t ever 1.15, current -0.69), and the falsifier is currently REFUTED on both legs — an orthogonal stream with negative OOS expected return is a zero-to-negative contributor, so CUT as a funded sleeve; correct posture is the spec's own zero-weight dormant tripwire.

spec.name: Turn-of-Month Broad-Equity Flow Premium (calendar overlay, beta-neutralized)
spec.mechanism: Documented mechanism: predictable month-end/month-start liquidity flows — pension/401k contributions, fund reinvestment of dividends/coupons, and 'infrequent rebalancing / risk-deferral' (recent JBF work) — systematically bid up equities in the ~8-day window spanning the last 1 and first 3 trading days of each month (≈+10bps/day vs ≈0bps elsewhere across 30 countries 1994-2023). It is a flow/structural effect, not a curve-fit. Reported HONESTLY as near-zero because in the actual 11y US large-cap OHLCV the premium has decayed: naive in-window SPY Sharpe fell from 1.23 IS to 0.57 OOS, and the market-neutral (long-in-window / scaled-short-out) overlay was -0.18 OOS. It is included as the family's second sleeve only as a low-turnover, near-zero diversifier with a hard falsifier — NOT as a Sharpe contributor.
spec.exactSignal: For each month build the trading-day index from aligned closes: tdom = position from month start (1-based), tdomEnd = position from month end (1-based). Define inTOM = (tdom <= 3) OR (tdomEnd <= 1) — i.e. last trading day of the prior month through the 3rd trading day of the current month (≈19% of days). Beta-neutral overlay: on inTOM days hold SPY weight = +1.0; on non-TOM days hold SPY weight = -s where s = fracTOM/(1-fracTOM) ≈ 0.24, so the TIME-AVERAGE net SPY exposure ≈ 0 (measured ρ to SPY = -0.04 full, 0.00 OOS). The sleeve return is purely the in-window-minus-rest spread.
spec.entryExit: Two rebalances per month: go to +1.0 SPY at the close of the trading day before the last day of the month; flip to -0.24 SPY at the close of the 3rd trading day. ≈24 round trips/year on a single ultra-liquid ETF → cost drag is small but, per the data, so is the edge.
spec.instruments: SPY only (single ultra-liquid ETF; long and scaled-short via the signed-weight engine). No single-name or HTB-short exposure.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Negligible — single broad-index ETF, no cross-sectional name selection, no delisting/borrow exposure. Construction is pure calendar timing so it cannot benefit from survivorship at all.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily log: inTOM flag, current SPY weight, sleeve daily return, cumulative PnL, trailing-250d in-window vs out-window mean-return gap and its t-stat, and realized net beta (should hover near 0). Monthly: recompute the in-minus-out spread; alert if the trailing-3y spread turns negative.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data: REFUTED (and it currently IS, in this dataset) if the mean daily SPY return inside the TOM window is not statistically greater than outside (one-sided t<1.3), OR if the beta-neutral overlay's OOS net-of-cost Sharpe <= 0 (measured -0.18 → fails today). Concretely: ship this sleeve ONLY if a rolling 3-year in-vs-out spread re-establishes positivity with t>1.5; otherwise it stays at zero weight as a monitored, non-allocated diversifier.
spec._family: Calendar/flow effects with a documented mechanism (FOMC information cycle + turn-of-month liquidity flow), constructed market-neutral and judged ruthlessly on post-2021 OOS net-of-cost Sharpe

scored.name: Slope-Timed Duration Carry (IEF vs SHY, roll-down-gated)
scored.family: Cross-asset carry (bond-curve & vol term-structure) via ETFs

scores.name: Slope-Timed Duration Carry (IEF vs SHY, roll-down-gated)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced exactly on repo data: it is trend-filtered long-duration (on-state rho to TLT=0.93, net +3.30 duration-years; the 0.40 k-cap makes dur-neutrality impossible, needs k=3.68), the full-sample 0.28 Sharpe is a 2016-2020 falling-rate artifact, OOS post-2021 after-cost Sharpe -0.44 / net-of-beta -0.45 trips the spec's own falsifier; low corr-to-book is orthogonality to a money-losing stream; costs/backtestability are fine but cannot rescue a negative-expectancy, sign-inverting signal.

spec.name: Slope-Timed Duration Carry (IEF vs SHY, roll-down-gated)
spec.mechanism: Bond-curve carry + roll-down (term premium): an upward-sloping Treasury curve pays the holder of the 7-10y belly (IEF) a positive carry (higher yield) plus roll-down (price appreciation as the bond ages down a positively-sloped curve) versus the ~2y short end (SHY). Documented risk premium (term premium; Ilmanen, 'Expected Returns'; AQR 'Carry' Koijen-Moskowitz-Pedersen-Vrugt 2018). It is a RATES premium, structurally distinct from equity momentum. The repair vs the prior internal RESEARCH_ONLY verdict and vs 2022: only harvest when the carry signal is actually positive AND the belly is trending up, so the sleeve sits flat through the inverted-and-falling-duration regime (2022) that destroys naive always-long-duration.
spec.exactSignal: Daily from adjusted closes of IEF and SHY (both 2766 bars, full 11y). Carry/roll-down proxy = trailing 63d total return of IEF minus trailing 63d total return of SHY (the realized slope-plus-roll spread; a positive-curve, positive-roll regime shows IEF outperforming SHY on a trend basis). Duration scale k = (vol of SHY daily returns over 60d)/(vol of IEF daily returns over 60d), capped to [0.15,0.40] (SHY ~1.9y vs IEF ~7y duration; k normalizes risk so the pair is duration-balanced, not a directional rate bet). Trend filter: IEF close > its 100d SMA (curve carry only collectible when the belly is not in a falling-rate-loss regime).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 21 trading days. If 63d(IEF) - 63d(SHY) > 0 AND IEF > 100d SMA: weight IEF = +0.50, weight SHY = -0.50*k (duration-balanced, dollar gross ~0.50-0.70, near duration-neutral so it isolates the carry/roll spread, not the level of rates). Else (inverted/negative-carry OR IEF below 100d SMA, e.g. most of 2022): flat (hold cash, no carry drag). Kill-switch already implicit: when IEF trend breaks the sleeve goes to cash, sidestepping the synchronized stocks-and-bonds-down tail the prior fleet flagged as the book's biggest residual risk.
spec.instruments: Long IEF, short SHY (both deeply liquid Treasury ETFs, tiny spread, SHY borrow is easy/cheap unlike single-name HTB shorts).
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low. Two index ETFs that have existed the whole window; no single-name selection, no extreme-decile bet, no delisting risk. Immune to the survivorship bias that inflated the prior single-name deciles.
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day log: the 63d IEF-SHY carry spread, IEF vs 100d SMA state (on/off), realized k, and the sleeve's daily P&L vs its 60d rolling Sharpe. Falsify-on-the-fly: if the on-state spread P&L 126d rolling Sharpe drops below 0 for 2 consecutive months, the allocator's sharpeFloor benches it (same mechanism that starved reversal to 4-7%).
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data: if the strategy's net-of-beta (vs SPY) after-cost Sharpe is <= 0.15, OR its realized correlation to the existing momentum-heavy book exceeds 0.25 (i.e. it is secretly a duration/risk-on beta bet), the sleeve is refuted and not funded. Specifically: if during 2022 the 'flat' filter fails to keep the sleeve out (i.e. it still draws down > 4% in the H1-2022 duration selloff), the roll-down-gate is not working and the thesis is rejected.
spec._family: Cross-asset carry (bond-curve & vol term-structure) via ETFs

scored.name: BAB-Sectors (beta-neutral betting-against-beta on the 11 SPDR sector ETFs)
scored.family: Cross-sectional low-idiosyncratic-vol / betting-against-beta L/S

scores.name: BAB-Sectors (beta-neutral betting-against-beta on the 11 SPDR sector ETFs)
scores.oneLineRationale: All 4 lenses replicated the exact spec on the real cache: gross net-of-beta Sharpe only 0.10 full/0.19 OOS (no premium before cost), full-sample net-of-beta NEGATIVE, OOS ~0.08 trips its own 0.15 falsifier, and it survives leave-2022-out at -0.55 nbSharpe/-14.9%; collapsing 1000s of BAB names to 11 sectors deletes the dispersion that powered the premium along with the survivorship bias (inseparable), and the result is a near-static long-XLU/XLP/XLV/short-XLK bet collinear with the funded low_vol sleeve (true rho ~0.30-0.40, shared 0.25 crash downside beta), not the claimed 0.05; cost/survivorship are genuinely clean (beta 0.02-0.05, ~30bps borrow) but cheap borrow on a zero edge is still no edge, and the repo already CUT this exact XLY/XLP cousin at 25/60/-0.15.

spec.name: BAB-Sectors (beta-neutral betting-against-beta on the 11 SPDR sector ETFs)
spec.mechanism: Frazzini-Pedersen (2014) betting-against-beta: leverage-constrained investors (mutual funds, retail) cannot use leverage so they overweight high-beta names to reach return targets, bidding high-beta prices up and depressing their risk-adjusted return; the security market line is too flat. Expressed at the GICS-sector level (XLU/XLP/XLV are structurally low-beta, XLK/XLY/XLF high-beta) the same flat-SML premium is captured with ZERO single-name survivorship (sectors are never delisted) and trivial borrow (sector SPDRs are deep, easy-to-borrow ~30bps not 150bps). Rank-weight + leverage-scale each leg so portfolio beta is ~0, which is the canonical BAB construction (long-leg scaled up by 1/beta_L, short-leg scaled down by 1/beta_H).
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for each of the 11 SPDR sector ETFs {XLK,XLF,XLV,XLE,XLI,XLY,XLP,XLB,XLU,XLC,XLRE} compute beta to SPY over a trailing 252-day window using simpleReturns + the existing beta() helper (XLRE/XLC have full history post-2015/2018; require >=200 obs). Rank sectors by ascending beta. LONG the bottom-half-by-beta (lowest ~5-6 sectors), SHORT the top-half (highest ~5-6). Within each leg, weight proportional to the cross-sectional rank-demeaned beta (Frazzini-Pedersen z_i = rank_i - mean(rank); long leg gets +|z| on low-beta, short leg -|z| on high-beta). Then scale the LONG leg by 1/beta_longLeg and the SHORT leg by 1/beta_shortLeg (portfolio-weighted betas) so net portfolio beta ~= 0; finally normalize gross to 1.0. Rebalance every 21 trading days; positions drift between.
spec.entryExit: Enter/refresh signed target weights at each 21-day rebalance EOD close from the beta ranks above; the weight engine charges 6bps round-trip per side on the turnover delta + daily borrow on the short notional (sector ETFs ~30bps, far below the 150bps default — model conservatively at default 150bps and it still survives because gross is small and turnover low). No discretionary exits; the beta rank IS the signal and it mean-reverts slowly.
spec.instruments: The 11 GICS SPDR sector ETFs (SECTOR_ETFS) long+short, SPY for beta estimation only (not traded).
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low / essentially none — the entire construction is sector ETFs, which are not subject to single-name delisting or survivorship bias; this is the explicit point of the design vs raw extreme-decile single-name BAB. Borrow is also genuine and cheap on sector SPDRs.
spec.dailyFeedback: Recompute 252d sector betas each day and log: (a) realized portfolio beta to SPY (should hover ~0; alarm if |beta|>0.15 — a neutralization failure), (b) which sectors are long vs short, (c) rolling 60d sleeve Sharpe and its rolling correlation to SPY and to the resid-mom L/S sleeve. A daily plot of long-leg-beta vs short-leg-beta confirms the SML spread the premium feeds on is still present.
spec.falsifier: Run walk-forward on post-2021-06 OOS data: if the sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe is <= 0.15 OOS, OR its realized portfolio beta to SPY exceeds 0.20 in absolute value (construction broken), OR its OOS correlation to the existing book exceeds 0.30, it is refuted and not funded. Specifically falsified if the low-beta-minus-high-beta sector spread earns a NEGATIVE net-of-beta return over 2022-2025 (the flat-SML premium has inverted/decayed at the sector level).
spec._family: Cross-sectional low-idiosyncratic-vol / betting-against-beta L/S

scored.name: Style-Spread Mean-Reversion L/S (factor/style convergence)
scored.family: Factor-momentum L/S on factor ETFs + factor-timing (extended to factor-reversion and defensive-factor relative-strength)

scores.name: Style-Spread Mean-Reversion L/S (factor/style convergence)
scores.oneLineRationale: Exact construct already built, faithfully backtested on the repo's own 11y cache, and CUT (IWF/IWD afterCost Sharpe -0.40, XLY/XLP -0.15); ln(IWF/IWD) is near-unit-root (AR1 rho=0.9994, HL ~4.7yr, VRs ~1.0) so there is no 1-3mo reversion to harvest, the -z signal fades a structural growth-over-value trend (a negative factor-momentum bet), corr-to-SPY +0.30-0.47 trips the spec's own 0.25 falsifier with residual long-beta that POSITIVELY correlates to the momentum book in stress, and the executed post-2021 net-of-beta Sharpe <=0 fires the spec's own kill criterion; backtestability/cost/implementability score high (4 cheap liquid ETFs, GC borrow, no survivorship) but there is zero gross edge for those virtues to protect.

spec.name: Style-Spread Mean-Reversion L/S (factor/style convergence)
spec.mechanism: Documented style-spread mean-reversion + short-term reversal of FACTOR/style returns (NY Fed SR513; Quantpedia short-term reversal; Klement). Relative performance between paired style ETFs that track overlapping baskets (growth vs value, cyclical vs defensive) is mean-reverting at the 1-3 month horizon: when one style sharply outperforms its pair, flows/crowding overshoot and the spread converges. Because the signal is the REVERSAL of recent relative strength, the stream is sign-opposite to the momentum-heavy book by construction (the documented economic reason is overreaction/liquidity-provision in style flows, not a price trend).
spec.exactSignal: Use the composition-anchored pairs already in code (STYLE_PAIRS): A=IWF vs B=IWD (growth/value), and A=XLY vs B=XLP (cyclical/defensive). For each pair compute the daily log relative-return series r_t = ln(closeA_t/closeA_{t-1}) - ln(closeB_t/closeB_{t-1}). Form the cumulative relative spread over a 63-day (3-month) window and take its z-score vs the trailing 252-day mean/stdev of the 63-day spread: z = (spread_63 - mean_252)/std_252. SIGNAL = -z (fade the stretch). Enter only when |z|>=1.0 (avoid noise band). Position: weight_A = clamp(-z,-1,1)*g, weight_B = -weight_A, with g sized so each pair runs ~0.5 gross; equal-weight the active pairs; total gross capped at 1.0, dollar-neutral per pair. Both legs are highly liquid ETFs that track overlapping S&P/Russell baskets so the pair is structurally beta-/sector-balanced (low survivorship sensitivity -- no single-name extreme-decile bets).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 5 trading days (weekly). Enter/scale toward target weight when |z|>=1.0; flatten a pair to 0 when |z| falls back below 0.5 (hysteresis band realizes convergence and caps turnover). Hard stop: if a pair's spread z-score keeps widening past |z|>=3 for 2 consecutive rebalances, cut that pair to zero for 21 days (regime-break guard against a true secular style rotation).
spec.instruments: IWF, IWD, XLY, XLP (4 liquid ETFs, ~1-2bp spreads). Long+short signed weights via existing weight engine; minimal/no borrow cost (easy-to-borrow large ETFs, well under the 150bp HTB rate).
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low. Only 4 large liquid ETFs that have existed the full sample; no delisting/extreme-decile exposure; pairs are composition-anchored so the L/S is structurally sector/beta-balanced rather than a survivorship-prone single-name extreme bet.
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day log per-pair z-score, target vs held weights, realized pair PnL, and rolling 63d pair Sharpe. Daily check: realized correlation of the sleeve return to xs_momentum/resid_momentum should stay <=0; if 21d realized ρ to the momentum book rises above +0.3, the reversion signal has flipped into trend and the sleeve should be benched.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, the sleeve's net-of-cost, net-of-beta Sharpe is <=0 (or its 21d realized correlation to the existing momentum book is persistently >+0.2). Concretely: if longing the laggard / shorting the leader at |z|>=1 does NOT produce positive convergence PnL after 6bp round-trip costs over 2022-2026, the style-spread reversion mechanism is dead and the sleeve is dropped.
spec._family: Factor-momentum L/S on factor ETFs + factor-timing (extended to factor-reversion and defensive-factor relative-strength)

scored.name: Short-Term Residual Reversal (1-week), sector- & beta-neutral L/S
scored.family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S

scores.name: Short-Term Residual Reversal (1-week), sector- & beta-neutral L/S
scores.oneLineRationale: Genuinely orthogonal and cleanly REAL_OHLCV-backtestable, but two independent in-engine implementations find it negative even gross (full +0.11/+0.13, OOS -0.11/-0.57) and ~-0.7 to -1.16 net-of-beta OOS with measured turnover ~7,800%/yr (4x the claimed <40%/wk); it trips every clause of its own falsifier and is dominated by the already-registered 21-day resid_momentum (net-of-beta +0.41, ~10x lower turnover) that hits the same momentum-offset goal — an uncorrelated stream that reliably loses money adds negative EV.

spec.name: Short-Term Residual Reversal (1-week), sector- & beta-neutral L/S
spec.family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S
spec.mechanism: Behavioral/microstructure: short-horizon reversal (Jegadeesh 1990, Lehmann 1990) is liquidity provision / overreaction correction. Raw 1-week reversal is cost-killed and survivorship-fragile (it loads on the most volatile, hardest-to-borrow names). Blitz-Huij-Martens (2011) show the RESIDUAL version — reverse only the part of last week's return NOT explained by market beta and the stock's own sector — is far cleaner: robust in every decade 1929-2008 and POSITIVE after costs even in the 500/100 largest stocks. Residualizing on sector is precisely what removes the survivorship/concentration sensitivity the family brief calls for, because the bet becomes intra-sector idiosyncratic, not 'short whatever sector just ran'. This is the SHORT end of the momentum-horizon spectrum and is the ONLY horizon with NEGATIVE correlation to the existing 12-1 momentum book.
spec.exactSignal: For each equity s (isEquity, ~165 names): r5(s) = simpleReturns(closes) summed/compounded over the last 5 trading days (1 week). Build a static GICS sector map from the universe.ts comment groupings (Tech/Financials/Healthcare/Staples/Discretionary/Energy/Industrials/Utilities/Materials/Communication; large-cap GICS sector is point-in-time-stable so this is not look-ahead). Residualize: (a) beta-adjust using the existing beta() on 60d daily returns vs SPY → resid_b = r5(s) - beta_s * r5(SPY); (b) sector-demean → residReversalScore(s) = -(resid_b - mean(resid_b over s's sector members)). Negative sign = we LONG the worst 1-week residual performers, SHORT the best. Rank cross-sectionally, LONG bottom-quintile-by-raw-resid (top by score), SHORT top, equal-weight, dollar-neutral (gross~1.0, 0.5 long / 0.5 short). Quintile (not decile) to cut survivorship/concentration sensitivity.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 5 trading days (weekly), NOT daily — the decisive cost control vs the benched RSI-2 st_reversal. Enter equal-weight quintile L/S on the residual-reversal score; fully refresh each week (a name exits when it leaves the extreme quintile, mechanically realizing the bounce). All signal inputs use data through i-1 (no-look-ahead, per the audited portfolio.ts convention).
spec.instruments: ~165 large/mid-cap US equities (EQUITY_UNIVERSE) for the cross-section; SPY for the beta residualization. Long + short signed weights via the weight engine; 150bps borrow charged daily on the short quintile.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: LOW-MEDIUM: sector-demeaning + beta-neutral + quintiles (not deciles) make it an intra-sector idiosyncratic bet rather than an extreme single-name bet, so it is much less survivorship-exposed than raw decile reversal — but the long leg can still tilt toward recently-beaten-down volatile names, so survivorship is non-zero (hence the honest haircut to s~0.45 and weekly, not daily, turnover).
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: track the realized P&L of the current weekly L/S book and its rolling 60d correlation to xs_momentum, resid_momentum, and SPY. Weekly: log realized turnover (target <40%/wk gross) and per-side cost drag; if net-of-cost weekly alpha < 0 for 8 consecutive weeks the edge has decayed.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021-06 OOS data, the sector+beta-neutral weekly residual-reversal L/S earns net-of-cost (6bps round-trip + 150bps borrow) cumulative return <= 0, OR its OOS net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2, OR its realized ρ to the existing momentum book is > +0.1 (i.e. it is NOT a distinct/negative stream). Any one refutes the sleeve.
spec._family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S

scored.name: Style/Cross-Asset Cointegrated-Pair Spread Reversion (z-score band, beta-hedged L/S)
scored.family: Cointegration pairs / stat-arb (within-sector + ETF pairs), market-neutral

scores.name: Style/Cross-Asset Cointegrated-Pair Spread Reversion (z-score band, beta-hedged L/S)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced the exact spec on real OHLCV (scripts/backtest/_redteam_coint_pairs.mjs): OOS net-of-beta Sharpe -1.23, mean per-trade P&L -0.67%, ADF gate passes only 14-33% of OOS days, and it is even negative in-sample (-0.30) and gross/gate-free, so the reversion mechanism is genuinely absent (these style/macro spreads are near-unit-root and TREND through the bands); the repo already CUT the identical IWF/IWD (-0.40) and XLY/XLP (-0.15) pairs; orthogonality is the only true claim (corr-to-book ~0.03) but a structurally negative-Sharpe, uncorrelated stream only dilutes the book.

spec.name: Style/Cross-Asset Cointegrated-Pair Spread Reversion (z-score band, beta-hedged L/S)
spec.family: Cointegration pairs / stat-arb (cointegrated ETF pairs), market-neutral
spec.mechanism: Economically-LINKED ETF pairs whose price ratio is cointegrated by construction share a common fundamental driver, so the log-spread is stationary and mean-reverts (Engle-Granger; cointegration-method pairs trading, which the literature shows beats the distance method AFTER costs). Pairs chosen for a STRUCTURAL link, not data-mined correlation: IWF/IWD (growth vs value — both Russell 1000 subsets, the same stocks split by style), GLD/SLV and GLD/GDX (gold vs silver vs gold-miners — same precious-metal driver), TLT/IEF (long vs intermediate Treasuries — same rates curve), XLY/XLP (cyclical vs defensive consumer). Mispricings come from style-rotation overshoot, metal lease-rate / beta-of-miners noise, and curve-flow dislocations — all flow-driven and reverting. The book is momentum/trend; a contrarian spread-reversion stream on style and macro spreads is near-orthogonal and slightly negatively correlated in trend reversals.
spec.exactSignal: For each structurally-linked pair (A,B) in {IWF/IWD, GLD/SLV, GLD/GDX, TLT/IEF, XLY/XLP}: compute log-spread L_t = ln(P_A,t) - h*ln(P_B,t), where the hedge ratio h is the 120-day OLS slope of ln(P_A) on ln(P_B) (re-estimated each rebalance; this is the cointegration vector, making the pair dollar-balanced in log space). Z-score z_t = (L_t - mean_{60d}(L)) / std_{60d}(L). Only TRADE a pair when the in-sample 120d Engle-Granger ADF p-value on the spread residual < 0.10 (cointegration gate — drop pairs that have de-cohered, the literature's #1 failure mode).
spec.entryExit: Enter when |z| > 2: short the rich leg, long the cheap leg, sized so the dollar legs are balanced by h and beta-neutral to SPY (scale the net pair by its 60d SPY beta so the combined book has ~0 market exposure). Equal risk budget across active pairs, gross=1.0 total. Exit/flatten the pair when |z| < 0.5 (reversion captured) or |z| > 4 (cointegration broken — stop out). Rebalance check daily but only trade on band crossings; effective turnover is low (pairs sit until they hit a band). Warmup 150 bars.
spec.instruments: 5 liquid ETF pairs: IWF/IWD, GLD/SLV, GLD/GDX, TLT/IEF, XLY/XLP. All large, penny-spread, easy-to-borrow (~30-60bps). No single-name idiosyncratic-news risk; GDX is the only mild borrow cost and still liquid.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Low. Only long-lived major ETFs; no delisting/IPO survivorship. The ADF cointegration gate is computed walk-forward on trailing data only (no look-ahead), and pairs that de-cohere are dropped rather than force-traded, which is the honest defense against the in-sample-only cointegration that inflates naive backtests.
spec.dailyFeedback: Per pair log: current z-score, walk-forward ADF p-value (cointegration health), hedge ratio h, beta hedge, days-in-trade, realized spread P&L, and rolling sleeve Sharpe + ρ to book. The ADF p-value time series is the early-warning: rising p-values mean cointegration is decaying and the pair should be benched before it loses money.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data: the sleeve's net-of-cost beta-neutral Sharpe <= 0.15, OR for the majority of pairs the trailing-120d ADF p-value stays > 0.10 across OOS (the spreads are not actually cointegrated out-of-sample, so the mechanism is absent), OR mean per-trade P&L after the 2-band round-trip cost is <= 0. Any one kills it.
spec._family: Cointegration pairs / stat-arb (within-sector + ETF pairs), market-neutral

scored.name: Sector-SPDR Relative-Value Reversion (basket-residual z-score, sector-neutral L/S)
scored.family: Cointegration pairs / stat-arb (within-sector + ETF pairs), market-neutral

scores.name: Sector-SPDR Relative-Value Reversion (basket-residual z-score, sector-neutral L/S)
scores.oneLineRationale: Negative GROSS of cost (OOS -0.28 to -0.44) before turnover even matters: the contrarian sign is backwards (sector-SPDR residuals mildly continue, lag-1 autocorr +0.02; the '3d half-life' is an overlapping-window summation artifact), so basket aggregation cancels the single-name over-reaction the thesis needs; ~4,650%/yr turnover on 3.3% vol then drives net OOS to -0.6/-1.5, triggering the spec's own falsifier (OOS<=0.2); orthogonality (rho~0.05, SPY-beta~0) and survivorship/borrow are genuinely clean and the REAL_OHLCV backtest fully reproduces on cached data, but low correlation to a negative-Sharpe stream is dilution not diversification, and this is a verbatim re-attempt of the already-CUT dollar-neutral ETF-spread / executed-reversal-null family (strategy-universe 'cost 2', red-team lines 23-28).

spec.name: Sector-SPDR Relative-Value Reversion (basket-residual z-score, sector-neutral L/S)
spec.mechanism: Documented short-horizon over-reaction / liquidity-provision premium at the SECTOR level (Avellaneda-Lee 2010 'Statistical Arbitrage in the US Equities Market' residual-reversion; Lehmann/Lo-MacKinlay contrarian). Sector SPDRs share the common market factor by construction, so the cross-sector spread is stationary around the market; when one sector dislocates from the basket on flow/noise (ETF-rebalance flows, single-name headlines bleeding into a whole sector, index reconstitution), it reverts over 3-10 days as liquidity providers are compensated for absorbing the imbalance. Trading the BASKET (not single names) makes convergence robust to idiosyncratic news — the documented reason ETF pairs decayed less than single-name pairs. This is contrarian in the cross-section of sectors, so it is structurally opposite-signed to the book's momentum/trend sleeves.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, on the 11 GICS sector SPDRs {XLK,XLF,XLV,XLE,XLI,XLY,XLP,XLB,XLU,XLC,XLRE}. For each sector i compute its log return r_i,t. Build the equal-weight basket return r_bar_t = mean over the 11 sectors. Beta-adjust each sector to the basket via 60-day OLS beta b_i (so each leg is basket-neutral, removing the common market factor). Residual e_i,t = r_i,t - b_i*r_bar_t. Cumulate the residual over a 5-day window S_i = sum_{k=t-4..t} e_i,k, then z_i = (S_i - mean_{20d}(S_i)) / std_{20d}(S_i). Signal weight is the cross-sectionally demeaned negative z-score (contrarian): w_raw_i = -(z_i - mean_j z_j). Long the sectors with the most-negative z (oversold vs basket), short the most-positive z.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 3 trading days (rebalanceDays=3) to keep turnover/costs sane while capturing the 3-10d reversion. Set signed weights w_i = 0.5 * w_raw_i / sum_j|w_raw_j| so gross=1.0, dollar-neutral (weights sum to ~0 after demeaning). Hard cap |w_i|<=0.20. Skip any sector with <80 bars or std=0. No stop; position decays as z mean-reverts and is re-set each cycle. Warmup 90 bars (needs 60d beta + 20d z window).
spec.instruments: 11 sector SPDR ETFs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLB/XLU/XLC/XLRE) — all penny-spread, deeply liquid, easy-to-borrow (~30-50bps). No single names, so NO HTB borrow blowups and minimal survivorship exposure.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low. Trades only 11 long-lived sector ETFs (no delisting, no single-name survivorship). The only mild bias is that XLC/XLRE were created mid-sample (2018/2015) — handled by skipping sectors lacking 80 bars, so no synthesized history.
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day log: per-sector residual z-score, signed target weights, realized 1-day and 3-day spread P&L per leg, gross/net exposure, rolling 60d sleeve Sharpe and ρ to the existing book. Track the median absolute z at entry and the realized reversion half-life; if half-life drifts above ~8 days the edge is decaying.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, the sleeve's net-of-cost, dollar-neutral return has annualized Sharpe <= 0.2 (95% CI excludes 0.3), OR the estimated reversion half-life of the residual spread exceeds 12 trading days (no exploitable mean reversion), OR ρ to the existing momentum/trend book exceeds 0.3 in OOS. Any one refutes it.

scored.name: Defensive-Factor Relative-Strength L/S (BAB/QMJ in a factor-ETF wrapper, vol-gated)
scored.family: Factor-momentum L/S on factor ETFs + factor-timing (extended to factor-reversion and defensive-factor relative-strength)

scores.name: Defensive-Factor Relative-Strength L/S (BAB/QMJ in a factor-ETF wrapper, vol-gated)
scores.oneLineRationale: Re-skin of already-CUT strategy-universe rows 25 (BAB L/S) and 14 (QMJ+lowvol L/S): beta-neutralizing a 4-ETF basket strips out the very low-beta channel through which the defensive premium pays, leaving a disguised short-growth/short-duration macro bet that won 2022 by luck and fails leave-one-crisis-out; real-OHLCV replication gives full-sample Sharpe -0.19 to -0.41 and OOS net ~0, firing BOTH of the spec's own falsifiers (OOS net-of-beta Sharpe <=0; vol gate worse than always-on, 0.16 vs 0.23) — the claimed 0.35 net-beta Sharpe is unsupported, the ~0.05 corr is real but it's low-rho noise, the gate churns ~750% turnover on a median-0.19 position, and the only non-losing variant (always-on, ~0.15) is redundant with the shipped long-only lowvol.ts core.

spec.name: Defensive-Factor Relative-Strength L/S (BAB/QMJ in a factor-ETF wrapper, vol-gated)
spec.mechanism: Betting-Against-Beta (Frazzini-Pedersen 2014) and Quality-Minus-Junk (Asness-Frazzini-Pedersen) premia: leverage-constrained investors overpay for high-beta/lottery exposure, so low-vol+quality outperform high-beta+junk on a risk-adjusted basis, especially when realized vol is elevated. Expressed as a beta-neutral L/S across DEFENSIVE factor ETFs (USMV min-vol, QUAL quality) vs AGGRESSIVE ones (SIZE small-size, IWF growth), it harvests a premium that is economically distinct from price momentum -- and the vol-regime gate is the documented factor-timing angle (BAB/QMJ pay most in stress, near-zero in calm bull trends), which keeps it from being a permanent short-beta drag.
spec.exactSignal: Defensive basket D = {USMV, QUAL}; Aggressive basket G = {SIZE, IWF}. Beta-neutralize: estimate each ETF's 60d beta to SPY; build D and G as equal-weight baskets, then scale the short (G) leg so basket betas match (w_G = w_D * betaD/betaG) -> net market beta ~0. Base position: LONG D, SHORT G at gross ~1.0. Factor-timing gate: let v = 20d realized vol of SPY (annualized) and vmed = its trailing 252d median. Scale gross exposure by g = clip((v/vmed - 0.8)/0.7, 0, 1.5) so the sleeve is near-flat in calm low-vol regimes and scales up (to ~1.5x within the engine's gross cap) when vol is elevated -- the regime where BAB/QMJ historically pays. Optionally add a relative-strength tilt: if 126d return of G basket > D basket by >15%, halve exposure (don't fight an extreme risk-on melt-up).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 21 trading days (monthly) to control turnover; recompute betas, baskets, and the vol gate each rebalance. Enter when g>0 (vol above the calm threshold); flatten to ~0 when g hits 0 (calm regime). No single-name stops needed -- baskets are diversified ETFs.
spec.instruments: USMV, QUAL (long) vs SIZE, IWF (short). All liquid factor ETFs, easy-to-borrow (borrow well under 150bp). Signed-weight L/S via existing engine; monthly turnover keeps costs low.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Low. Four broad factor ETFs spanning the full sample; no single stocks, no delisting risk; beta-neutralized basket-vs-basket construction is far less survivorship-sensitive than a raw bottom-decile single-name short.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily log: SPY 20d realized vol vs 252d median (the gate), basket betas + net portfolio beta (target ~0), D-vs-G 126d relative strength, gross exposure g, and sleeve PnL. Daily check: net beta should stay within +-0.1; if the vol gate is ON but the sleeve bleeds for >2 months, the defensive premium is not paying in this cycle.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, LONG defensive / SHORT aggressive (vol-gated, beta-neutral) delivers net-of-cost, net-of-beta Sharpe <=0 -- i.e., USMV+QUAL do NOT beat SIZE+IWF on a beta-matched basis during elevated-vol windows (e.g. fails across the 2022 drawdown and 2025 vol spikes). If the vol gate adds no improvement over an always-on version, the factor-timing thesis is also refuted and the sleeve is dropped.
spec._family: Factor-momentum L/S on factor ETFs + factor-timing (extended to factor-reversion and defensive-factor relative-strength)

scores.name: Relative-Value Cross-Asset Spread Trend (curve / metals-ratio / credit / oil-vs-commodity)
scores.oneLineRationale: Faithful REAL_OHLCV replication (verified: scripts/backtest/_redteam_rvspread_oos.ts, OOS net-beta Sharpe -0.10 vs claimed +0.38, 3/4 legs negative OOS, credit leg smuggles +0.20 SPY beta, 1303%/yr turnover, 0/27 swept configs clear 0.15, 2023 placebo beat the signal) fires the spec's own falsifier three ways and matches the repo red-team.md's standing rejection of all dollar-neutral spread sleeves on this thin cross-section; orthogonal-but-empty (rho to existing trend sleeve 0.18-0.32, not the claimed 0.02) with no surviving alpha to decorrelate.

spec.name: Relative-Value Cross-Asset Spread Trend (curve / metals-ratio / credit / oil-vs-commodity)
spec.family: Time-series momentum / managed futures on a BROAD multi-asset ETF set, vol-scaled
spec.mechanism: Trend applied to STRUCTURALLY BETA-ZERO intra-asset-class SPREADS rather than outright directional positions. The cited evidence (Quantpedia; tandfonline commodity TS-vs-XS) is explicit that relative-value trend in rates/FX/commodities is far less exposed to the single global equity factor than outright time-series trend — exactly the decorrelation lever the portfolio-Sharpe math needs. Each spread has its own documented trend mechanism: (1) yield-curve momentum / 'duration trend' (TLT-IEF and IEF-SHY steepener-flattener trends persist with the rate cycle — Fed hiking = flattener trend in 2022, easing = steepener); (2) gold/silver-ratio trend (SLV is higher-beta to the same monetary-debasement driver as GLD, so the GLD/SLV ratio trends with risk-appetite/industrial-demand regimes — a classic CTA spread); (3) credit-spread momentum (HYG-vs-IEF: HY excess return over duration-matched Treasuries trends with the credit cycle, documented credit-momentum anomaly); (4) commodity term-structure proxy (USO oil vs DBC broad basket trends with the energy-vs-ags/metals dispersion). Because each leg is long one ETF and short a closely-related ETF, the equity beta and the broad-trend factor net to ~0, leaving an almost pure idiosyncratic spread-trend stream — near-zero rho to BOTH the equity book and the existing directional trend sleeve.
spec.exactSignal: Four dollar-neutral spread legs, each weight 0.25 gross. For spread X=(A,B) form the log-ratio series r = ln(closeA) - ln(closeB). Trend score = sign( SMA(r,21) - SMA(r,100) ) i.e. fast-minus-slow MA crossover on the ratio (a real trend signal computable from indicators.ts: sma()). Position = +score on A, -score on B, each leg inverse-vol-weighted by annualizedVol of the SPREAD return (logReturns of r), normalized so each of the 4 spreads contributes equal risk and total gross=1.0, net=0. Spreads: (1) TLT vs IEF (curve duration trend), (2) GLD vs SLV (metals ratio trend), (3) HYG vs IEF (credit-spread trend), (4) USO vs DBC (energy term-structure trend). If any leg's |SMA-fast - SMA-slow| is within a small deadband (< 0.25 * spread vol), hold that leg flat to cut whipsaw.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 10 trading days (spreads are lower-vol so need slightly faster refresh than outrights, but 10d keeps turnover/borrow modest). Flip a spread when the fast-slow MA crossover changes sign and clears the deadband; flatten that spread inside the deadband.
spec.instruments: 6 ETFs forming 4 spreads: TLT/IEF (curve), GLD/SLV (metals ratio), HYG/IEF (credit), USO/DBC (energy term structure). All in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS. Short legs (IEF appears short in credit leg, SLV/DBC short when ratio downtrends) incur borrow; ~150bps on HTB names but these are liquid ETFs (low borrow).
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Negligible. Six large, liquid, never-delisted ETFs; every position is a hedged spread, not a single-name extreme-decile bet. No survivorship channel at all. Cost honesty: the 10-day rebalance roughly doubles turnover vs the 21-day sleeves, so the after-cost estimate already haircuts ~15-20bps/yr of two-sided spread costs; USO/SLV contango is embedded in adjusted closes.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily runner logs each spread's fast/slow SMA, current sign, and deadband state; tracks the 4 spread returns separately so a single broken leg (e.g. credit-spread trend stops working) can be pruned without killing the sleeve. Daily monitor: rolling 60d correlation of the combined sleeve to SPY (target ~0) and to cross_asset_trend (target ~0); alert if either > 0.25. Also monitor per-spread Sharpe to catch the curve leg dominating.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, the 4-spread sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe is <= 0.10, OR more than 3 of the 4 individual spread legs have negative OOS Sharpe (meaning the equal-risk basket is carried by one lucky leg and is not a robust relative-value-trend family), OR its realized correlation to SPY exceeds 0.25 (meaning the spreads are NOT beta-neutral in practice). Any of these refutes the structural-decorrelation thesis.

scored.name: IVOL-neutral L/S (rank-weighted low-minus-high idiosyncratic-vol, beta-hedged, single-name)
scored.family: Cross-sectional low-idiosyncratic-vol / betting-against-beta L/S

scores.name: IVOL-neutral L/S (rank-weighted low-minus-high idiosyncratic-vol, beta-hedged, single-name)
scores.oneLineRationale: Faithfully backtested through the repo's own audited engine the low-minus-high idioVol L/S is negative even gross of costs (OOS net-of-beta -0.4 to -0.67 vs claimed +0.38): the published Ang-Hodrick anomaly inverts in this 100%-survivor mega/large-cap universe because the high-IVOL short leg IS the decade's winners (NVDA/AMD/SMCI/COIN), the survivorship falsifier fires perversely (dropping the 10 newest shorts IMPROVES OOS), corr-to-resid_momentum 0.32-0.44 breaches its own 0.40 redundancy floor, and resid_momentum (net-of-beta +0.41) already strictly dominates it — CUT, keep at most the long low-idio leg already covered by the low_vol sleeve.

spec.name: IVOL-neutral L/S (rank-weighted low-minus-high idiosyncratic-vol, beta-hedged, single-name)
spec.mechanism: Ang-Hodrick-Xing-Zhang (2006/2009) idiosyncratic-volatility anomaly: stocks with high IDIOSYNCRATIC (market-residual) volatility earn abnormally LOW future returns — driven by lottery-preference/overpricing of high-vol names (behavioral) plus arbitrage-asymmetry (costly to short the overpriced high-IVOL leg). Distinct from the existing long-only low_vol sleeve (whose 0.10 net-of-beta shows it is mostly beta) and from BAB (total beta vs residual vol): here the signal is the volatility of the MARKET-RESIDUAL, run dollar- and beta-neutral so the alpha survives the CAPM. Distinct from resid_momentum (which ranks the LEVEL of residual return, not its VOLATILITY) though the short legs partially overlap.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for each of the ~165 equity-universe names with >=130 bars: regress 60d simpleReturns on SPY 60d returns (existing beta() helper), take residual series e_t = r_t - beta*spy_t, compute idioVol = stdev(e_t)*sqrt(252). Rank cross-sectionally. To CUT survivorship/borrow sensitivity do NOT use extreme deciles: take the bottom THIRD (low idioVol) long and top THIRD (high idioVol) short, RANK-WEIGHTED (weight prop to demeaned rank, capped at 3% per name so no single survivor/HTB name dominates). Make dollar-neutral (sum long = -sum short = 0.5 gross each, gross 1.0). Then beta-hedge: compute portfolio beta of the L/S book to SPY and overlay an SPY weight = -portfolioBeta so net beta ~=0 (or scale legs Frazzini-Pedersen style). Rebalance every 21 days (IVOL ranks are persistent 1-3 months per the literature, so turnover is moderate).
spec.entryExit: Signed weights refreshed at each 21-day rebalance EOD; engine charges 6bps/side on turnover + 150bps annual borrow on short notional (the high-IVOL short leg IS the hard-to-borrow cohort — COIN/AFRM/RIVN/CVNA/SMCI/MSTR — so 150bps is the right conservative rate and the 3% name cap limits concentration there). No discretionary exit.
spec.instruments: The ~165 single-name EQUITY_UNIVERSE long+short, rank-weighted thirds, SPY for residual/beta estimation and as the beta-hedge overlay.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Moderate — higher than BAB-Sectors. The high-IVOL SHORT leg is the survivorship-inflated, hard-to-borrow cohort the prior audit flagged (the universe is 100% survivors; names like SVB/FRC that blew up are absent, which biases the short leg's apparent profitability upward). Mitigated by rank-weighting (not extreme decile), a 3% per-name cap, and the 150bps borrow charge — but this sleeve's standalone Sharpe should be treated as an UPPER BOUND until point-in-time data is available. Ranked second behind BAB-Sectors for this reason.
spec.dailyFeedback: Log daily: (a) realized net beta after the SPY hedge (alarm if |beta|>0.15), (b) long-leg vs short-leg idioVol spread (the premium's fuel), (c) rolling 60d correlation to resid_momentum specifically — the short legs overlap so this is the binding correlation risk; if it climbs above 0.4 the sleeve is redundant, (d) borrow utilization / locate availability on the short leg as a live capacity check.
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward OOS post-2021-06: refuted and NOT funded if net-of-beta Sharpe <= 0.15 OOS, OR realized correlation to resid_momentum > 0.40 (redundant with an existing sleeve), OR the low-minus-high idioVol spread earns negative net return 2022-2025. Decisive survivorship falsifier: if removing the 10 most-recently-IPO'd / most-volatile short names collapses the OOS Sharpe by more than half, the edge was survivorship/borrow artifact, not anomaly — drop it.

scored.family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S

scores.name: Sector-Neutral Long-Term Residual Reversal L/S (36-12m)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced the spec harness on the 2015-2026 cache: honest OOS net-of-beta Sharpe is ~0.0 (post-2021 0.32 is ENTIRELY the 2022 crisis at 1.52; calm 2023-26 -0.18, 2024-26 -0.42, 2025-26 -0.34) — a disguised short-vol snapback, not the promised slow value diversifier, failing its own 0.15 falsifier; orthogonality is fatal (rho to resid_momentum -0.34 to -0.65, a re-packaged short of the book's best survivor that the positive-only +0.25 gate is blind to, plus +0.21 to +0.71 redundancy with existing lt_reversal which itself dominates at 0.51-0.88); 614%/yr turnover with double borrow on a near-canceling book kills cost robustness; the in-house sharpe2-quest.md already measured this exact family at 0.16, half the claimed 0.35; backtestable but uninvestable — CUT.

spec.name: Sector-Neutral Long-Term Residual Reversal L/S (36-12m)
spec.family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S
spec.mechanism: Behavioral overreaction (De Bondt-Thaler 1985): 3-5y losers beat winners as extrapolative mispricing slowly corrects — a price-implied VALUE proxy. Critically, web check shows the RAW effect has decayed in liquid large-caps; what survives after costs even in the 100-500 largest names is the RESIDUAL (systematic-stripped) component (~2.5x total reversal profit). So I strip each stock's SECTOR-ETF contribution before ranking: long the names that have lagged their own sector over the 36-12m window, short those that led it. This isolates the idiosyncratic mean-reversion the literature finds robust, and being slow/value-like it is structurally negatively correlated to the book's 12-1 momentum sleeves (the classic value-momentum diversification).
spec.exactSignal: For each equity s in EQUITY_UNIVERSE with >=760 bars: formationRet(s)=close[t-21]/close[t-756]-1 (36-month total return skipping the most recent 21 trading days to avoid contaminating with 1m short-term reversal). Map s to its sector ETF E(s) via GICS bucket (the universe is already sector-blocked; e.g. AAPL/MSFT->XLK, JPM/GS->XLF, XOM/CVX->XLE, UNH/JNJ->XLV, PG/KO->XLP, HD/MCD->XLY, CAT/HON->XLI, LIN/FCX->XLB, NEE/DUK->XLU, DIS/NFLX->XLC). secRet(s)=E_close[t-21]/E_close[t-756]-1. Beta b(s)=OLS of daily simpleReturns(s) on simpleReturns(E(s)) over trailing 252d. residReversalScore(s)= -(formationRet(s) - b(s)*secRet(s)) (sign flipped so a big NEGATIVE sector-residual return -> high score -> LONG). Rank all scores; long the top decile (biggest underperformers vs sector), short the bottom decile (biggest sector-relative winners). To suppress lottery/distress noise (web: reversals concentrate in junk), drop any name whose 60d annualizedVol is in the top 10% of the cross-section before ranking.
spec.entryExit: Equal-weight within each leg, dollar-neutral: long leg sums to +0.5, short leg to -0.5 (gross ~1.0). Rebalance every 21 trading days; a name leaves a leg when it falls out of its decile at the next rebalance. No stops (slow value signal). Borrow charged by engine on the short decile.
spec.instruments: ~100-165 large/mid-cap US equities (EQUITY_UNIVERSE) long+short; 11 sector SPDRs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLB/XLU/XLC/XLRE) used only as the neutralizing benchmark, not traded.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Medium — it IS an extreme-decile single-name bet, so dead/delisted losers (the very names a reversal strategy wants to be long) are absent from the cache, which BIASES the long-loser leg UPWARD; this is the main inflation risk. Mitigated (vs a raw DBT decile) by sector-neutralization, the high-vol/distress filter, dollar-neutral construction, and a liquid large-cap-only universe. Treat the in-sample Sharpe as an upper bound; the 0.35 estimate already haircuts for it.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: mark the L/S book to that day's closes, log leg returns, rolling 60d Sharpe, realized gross, and the 60d correlation of this sleeve's daily PnL to resid_momentum and xs_momentum (the two it must stay decorrelated from). Alert if 60d ρ to resid_momentum exceeds +0.4 (shared short book leaking) or if rolling 6m Sharpe < -0.3.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data, run the strategy AND a placebo that shuffles the sector-residual sign. If the real sleeve's OOS net-of-beta Sharpe is <=0.15, OR it is statistically indistinguishable from the sign-shuffled placebo, OR its OOS PnL correlation to the existing momentum book is >+0.25 (i.e. it is NOT the promised value-diversifier), the sleeve is refuted and dropped.
spec._family: Long-term reversal (3-5y) as a price-based VALUE proxy, L/S

spec.name: VIX Term-Structure Slope Switch (SVXY carry / VIXY tail-flip)
spec.mechanism: Volatility risk premium harvested via the VIX term-structure SLOPE (^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M), the cleanest fast carry/roll signal available. In steep contango (calm) front VIX futures roll down toward lower spot -> a small short-vol (SVXY) position earns roll yield (documented VRP; the same premium PutStrike harvests on the option chain). The decisive repair of the prior CUT 'SVXY contango' (score 17, naked short-gamma): this sleeve is SLOPE-STATE-CONDITIONAL and FLIPS to LONG vol (VIXY) the instant the curve backwardates (^VIX9D>^VIX), converting the sleeve's left tail from catastrophic (Volmageddon -80%/day) to CONVEX/positive-skew exactly when equities crash. So instead of ADDING short-gamma crash risk to the put/equity book (the fleet's #2 finding: tail-additivity), it is tail-ORTHOGONAL and even tail-hedging in stress — but distinct from tail_hedge (which buys TLT/GLD, not a vol ETP, and goes risk-off; here the calm-state carry leg is the return engine).
spec.exactSignal: Daily from ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M (all 2766 bars) and SVXY/VIXY adjusted closes (2766 bars, full window — avoid VXX which only starts 2018). Slope1 = ^VIX9D/^VIX; Slope2 = ^VIX/^VIX3M. Contango score = (1-Slope1) blended with (1-Slope2); require BOTH < 1 (clean contango) to be short-vol. Backwardation trigger = Slope1 > 1.0. Spike kill = ^VIX >= 35 (forces flat-or-long, never short into a spike, the Volmageddon guard).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 3 trading days (matches tail_hedge cadence; fast enough to flip before the bulk of a vol spike, slow enough to clear costs). State machine: (A) Clean contango (Slope1<0.95 AND Slope2<1.0 AND VIX<25): SHORT-vol via weight SVXY = +0.20 (SVXY is already 0.5x-levered post-2018, so +0.20 SVXY ~ 0.10 vega; deliberately SMALL and bounded — never scale the short leg up). (B) Backwardation (Slope1>1.0) OR VIX>=35: FLIP to LONG-vol via weight VIXY = +0.15 (convex crash payoff). (C) Mixed/transition: flat (cash). Gross capped at 0.25. The asymmetric sizing (long-vol leg sized for convexity, short-vol leg kept small) means expected P&L is modest but the tail is the RIGHT sign.
spec.instruments: Long SVXY in contango (no borrow — it is a long ETF position, not a short), long VIXY in backwardation/spike. No naked shorting, no options, no HTB borrow. Both ETPs span the full 11y.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Low-to-moderate. ETPs only, no single-name selection. The honest caveat: SVXY/VIXY are themselves products that have been re-geared (SVXY cut to 0.5x in 2018), so the pre-2018 short-vol leg behaves more aggressively than post-2018 — the backtest must use the as-traded series and the OOS judgment is the post-2021 sub-sample (entirely post-regearing, the conservative read). No delisting/decile survivorship.
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day log: Slope1, Slope2, the state (short-vol / flat / long-vol), VIX level, and sleeve daily P&L + 60d rolling Sharpe. Critical guard logged daily: realized correlation of this sleeve to the aggregate book on DOWN-SPY days (must stay <= 0 — i.e. it must not bleed when the book bleeds). If the down-day correlation turns positive for 21d, the tail-flip is mis-timed and the allocator de-grosses it.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS: refuted if net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe <= 0.10, OR if its DOWN-MARKET-day return correlation to the book is > 0 (the whole point is convex/negative tail co-movement — if it is positive, the short-vol leg is leaking into crashes and it is tail-ADDITIVE like the version the fleet correctly CUT). Hard refuter: if during any VIX>35 episode in the OOS window the sleeve posts a daily loss worse than -3% (it should be FLAT or LONG vol there, hence flat-to-positive), the state machine is broken and the sleeve is killed.
spec._family: Cross-asset carry (bond-curve & vol term-structure) via ETFs

scored.family: Novel uncorrelated streams (sector lead-lag spillover, intra-market realized-vol / overnight-intraday dispersion)

scores.name: Overnight-vs-Intraday Reversal (tug-of-war, beta-neutral L/S)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced on the repo's own 180-name cache: the Lou-Polk-Skouras edge lives in the intraday leg (IC -0.0105) but the close-to-close engine books the overnight-continuation leg (IC +0.0334), flipping the bookable signal to a statistically-zero +0.0053 (t=0.74) and negative-OOS; after-cost OOS Sharpe -0.13/-0.19/-0.30 at 0/150/400bps fails its own +0.15 falsifier, sole positive year is 2022 (leave-it-out = -0.03), turnover 66x/yr on an HTB survivor-only short decile, and the beta-balance rescale blows to 9.4x leverage; orthogonality to the book is real (-0.03) but it is orthogonality-without-alpha (a noise stream), and capturing the documented effect needs intraday open->close execution the EOD engine and brief.md forbid — CUT.

spec.name: Overnight-vs-Intraday Reversal (tug-of-war, beta-neutral L/S)
spec.mechanism: Documented: Lou-Polk-Skouras 'Tug of War' + NY Fed Overnight Drift (SR917). Retail/sentiment flow overnight pushes a name's CLOSE->OPEN (overnight) return up, then institutional arbitrage corrects it during the OPEN->CLOSE (intraday) session: overnight returns NEGATIVELY predict next-day intraday returns (and vice-versa). A stock with persistently strong overnight + weak intraday is retail-overbought and mean-reverts; the opposite is under-loved. This is a behavioral/structural clientele effect, not a curve-fit, documented to persist years post-formation.
spec.exactSignal: For each equity, decompose daily bars (ctx.bars(s) gives open & close) into: overnight_t = open_t / close_{t-1} - 1 and intraday_t = close_t / open_t - 1. Over a trailing 10-day window compute cumulative overnight return ON_s = sum(overnight) and cumulative intraday return ID_s = sum(intraday). Define the tug-of-war score TOW_s = ON_s - ID_s (high = overnight-driven / retail-overbought; low = intraday-driven / institutionally-favored). Beta-residualize is not needed on TOW itself, but for sizing compute each name's 60d beta to SPY. Rank TOW cross-sectionally: SHORT the top decile (overnight-overbought, expected to revert), LONG the bottom decile (intraday-strong / overnight-weak). Equal-weight each leg, dollar-neutral, and beta-balance the two legs (scale leg notionals so sum(beta_long*w) ~ sum(beta_short*w)) for net market beta ~0. CRITICAL HONESTY: positions are held CLOSE-TO-CLOSE (the engine marks close-to-close); the open is used ONLY to build the signal, never to book P&L — so the backtest is engine-faithful and not an un-executable overnight-capture fantasy.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 5 trading days (rebalanceDays:5) to control turnover (the raw signal is fast/noisy daily). Enter top/bottom-decile TOW L/S; a name rotates out when it leaves the extreme decile. Hold close-to-close. warmupBars ~80 (need ~60d beta + 10d split windows + buffer).
spec.instruments: ~150 EQUITY_UNIVERSE single names, signed weights, dollar- and beta-neutral L/S via the engine. SPY read for beta only.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: MEDIUM: it IS a decile single-name L/S, but the sort is on an OVERNIGHT/INTRADAY MICROSTRUCTURE signal that is orthogonal to 12-1 momentum (the dimension driving most survivorship in the book), so the dead-name bias differs in character from the momentum sleeves; beta-neutral construction and decile (not 1%%) cutoffs limit single-name blow-up. Mitigate further by capping name weight and excluding names with <60d of history.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: track the rolling IC between TOW score and next-5d close-to-close excess return; monitor the realized overnight-vs-intraday spread of the SPY/QQQ universe (the macro 'tug-of-war' regime) — the edge is strongest when the overnight drift is large and weakens as ETF after-hours create/redeem compresses it (documented decay). Alert if 60d IC turns negative or net beta |b|>0.1.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data: if the net-of-beta after-cost (5bps/side + 150bps borrow) OOS Sharpe is < 0.15, OR the 60-day rolling IC between TOW and next-5d return is negative for a majority of OOS months, OR realized correlation to the existing resid-momentum L/S sleeve exceeds 0.3 (it would no longer be a distinct stream), the sleeve is refuted and dropped.

scored.name: Residual 5-Day Reversal L/S (beta/vol-neutralized, weekly, liquidity-screened)
scored.family: Short-term reversal as MARKET-NEUTRAL L/S (long 1-5d losers, short winners), cost-aware

scores.name: Residual 5-Day Reversal L/S (beta/vol-neutralized, weekly, liquidity-screened)
scores.oneLineRationale: Real-OHLCV reconstruction shows the sign is backwards (liquid large-caps continue, not reverse): net-of-beta Sharpe -0.54 OOS post-2021, negative even gross of costs; the repo's own RSI-2/ResMom-LS/VIX-5d-reversal trials in this exact engine were all CUT (net-of-beta -0.16, 27/60, 22/60); all three of the spec's own falsifier conditions trip and survivorship inflates the loser-long leg, so it is a confirmed null not a diversifier.

spec.name: Residual 5-Day Reversal L/S (beta/vol-neutralized, weekly, liquidity-screened)
spec.family: Short-term reversal as MARKET-NEUTRAL L/S (long 1-5d losers, short winners), cost-aware
spec.mechanism: Liquidity-provision / overreaction premium (Lehmann 1990; Blitz-Huij-Martens 'Short-Term Residual Reversal' 2009/2012). Short-horizon price moves overshoot when uninformed order flow (forced liquidations, index/ETF flows, retail lottery demand) hits finite dealer/arbitrageur risk capacity; the L/S earns the compensation for absorbing that flow and the subsequent mean-reversion. Residualizing the 5d return on market beta (resid = r5_stock - beta*r5_SPY) and scaling by idiosyncratic vol removes the size/vol concentration that makes RAW reversal trade excessively in tiny, volatile names — Blitz-Huij-Martens show this doubles the risk-adjusted return and cuts trading costs, and it makes the premium covary with volatility (a genuine risk premium, not a curve-fit).
spec.exactSignal: Universe: the ~165 EQUITY_UNIVERSE large/mid-caps only (already a liquidity screen). Daily liquidity gate: require 60d median dollar volume (median of close[t]*volume[t] over last 60 bars) > $50M AND >= 250 valid bars of history; drop names failing the gate that day. For each surviving name compute the 5-day simple return r5 = close[t]/close[t-5]-1. Compute SPY 5-day return r5_SPY likewise. Estimate beta b over the trailing 60 daily returns vs SPY (existing beta() helper). Residual reversal score = -( r5_stock - b*r5_SPY ) / max(idioVol, 0.10), where idioVol = annualizedVol(close,20) (sign flipped so the BIGGEST recent residual LOSERS get the highest score = long). Standardize scores cross-sectionally to z-scores. Rank; long the top ~15% by score (recent residual losers), short the bottom ~15% (recent residual winners), equal-weight within each leg, dollar-neutral (sum longs=+0.5, sum shorts=-0.5, gross ~1.0). To cut turnover, only act if the name's z-score sign/magnitude crossed the entry band since last rebalance (hysteresis: enter top/bottom 15%, exit only when it leaves top/bottom 30%).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 5 trading days (weekly). Enter: name in top-15% residual-loser z (long) or bottom-15% residual-winner z (short) at the rebalance close. Hold 5 trading days. Exit/replace at next weekly rebalance; hysteresis band (exit only outside top/bottom 30%) suppresses churn on names hovering near the threshold. Equal-weight, dollar- and (approximately) beta-neutral by construction since the score is beta-residualized and longs~shorts.
spec.instruments: Long & short the ~165 large/mid-cap US equities in EQUITY_UNIVERSE (signed weights). SPY closes used only to compute beta/market residual, never traded. Engine charges 6bps round-trip + 150bps annual borrow on the short leg.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: LOW-MEDIUM. Beta/vol residualization plus the $50M dollar-volume screen keep the book OFF the smallest/most-fragile names where delisting/survivorship bias bites hardest, and dollar-neutral L/S means a delisted name hurts the long leg but a symmetric short cohort offsets index-wide survivorship drift. Still single-name (not ETF), so somewhat exposed to the fixed-survivor 165-name list — the universe excludes already-dead tickers, which biases the LONG (recent-loser) leg optimistically; honest Sharpe is haircut for this. Sector-neutralizing (Spec 2's refinement) would lower it further.
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day compute the realized 1-day P&L of the live weekly book and the spread between the long (residual-loser) and short (residual-winner) legs; track 20-day rolling net-of-cost Sharpe and the long-short spread decay curve (how much of the 5d bounce realized by day 1,2,3). Alert if realized borrow on the short leg exceeds the 150bps assumption (HTB names) or if 20d rolling spread goes negative for >3 consecutive weeks (premium crowded out).
spec.falsifier: On strict post-2021 OOS data with the conservative cost model (6bps round-trip + 150bps borrow, weekly rebalance), the sleeve is refuted if its net-of-beta annualized Sharpe is <= 0.25, OR its realized correlation to the existing book (xs_momentum + resid_momentum + cross_asset_trend + tail_hedge) exceeds +0.25 in absolute value, OR the long-minus-short residual-reversal spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t<1.5) net of costs. Any one of these kills it.
spec._family: Short-term reversal as MARKET-NEUTRAL L/S (long 1-5d losers, short winners), cost-aware

scored.name: FOMC-Cycle Risk-Appetite Sector Rotation (even/odd-week, cyclical-vs-defensive, beta-neutral)
scored.family: Calendar/flow effects with a documented mechanism (FOMC information cycle + turn-of-month liquidity flow), constructed market-neutral and judged ruthlessly on post-2021 OOS net-of-cost Sharpe

scores.name: FOMC-Cycle Risk-Appetite Sector Rotation (even/odd-week, cyclical-vs-defensive, beta-neutral)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced all four red-team probes on real cached OHLCV: the CMV even-week premium is INVERTED in 2015-2026 (SPY even-odd = -6.8bps), so the directional thesis is wrong-signed; sleeve is in-sample-negative (Shp -0.84, t_alpha -2.08) and OOS-flat (post-2021 beta-neutral Shp -0.015, alpha t 0.05), failing every one of the spec's own falsifiers; the "similar betas" claim is false (static +0.5XLY-0.5XLP beta=0.266, t=32) so apparent rho=0.004 hides ±0.27 calendar-timed conditional beta plus a shared crash tail (downside beta +0.06, t=3.24); turnover is understated ~6x (49.6 weekly flips/yr, no real "block"), the edge nets ~0 in a 2-5bps knife-edge, it is parity-fragile and LOCO-fragile, and the repo's own red-team.md already rejected XLY/XLP market-neutral pairs on this exact universe — backtestability is the only honest dimension; CUT.

spec.name: FOMC-Cycle Risk-Appetite Sector Rotation (even/odd-week, cyclical-vs-defensive, beta-neutral)
spec.mechanism: Documented in 'Stock Returns over the FOMC Cycle' (JF 2019): the entire post-1994 equity premium is earned in EVEN weeks (0,2,4,6 counted from each FOMC announcement), and is causally tied to the Fed's information-disclosure/liquidity-provision cycle (intermeeting target changes, funds-futures vol, internal Board meetings) — a risk-appetite cycle, NOT a macro-release artifact (even weeks do not line up with major data). I express it cross-sectionally as a risk-appetite tilt — long cyclicals / short defensives when the cycle is risk-on (even), reversed when risk-off (odd) — so it is dollar- and approximately beta-neutral and isolates the cycle, not market direction. This is distinct from (and survives where) the pre-FOMC 24h drift, which decayed to zero after 2015.
spec.exactSignal: Maintain a hardcoded list of scheduled FOMC announcement dates (8/yr, published years ahead; falsifiable & auditable). Each trading day t, find the most recent FOMC announcement on/before t, compute calendarDaysSince = floor((t - lastFOMC)/1day), week = floor(calendarDaysSince/7); isEven = (week % 2 == 0). SYMMETRIC beta-neutral construction: if isEven (risk-on) set weights {XLY:+0.5, XLP:-0.5}; if odd (risk-off) set {XLY:-0.5, XLP:+0.5}. Gross=1.0, dollar-neutral. (XLY and XLP have similar ETF betas so net beta is small; an optional refinement multiplies the XLY leg by betaXLP/betaXLY from a trailing-120d OLS to force net beta to 0.) Measured ρ of this symmetric stream to SPY = 0.004 full-sample — genuinely orthogonal.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance at each EOD close when the even/odd flag flips (≈6-8 effective block-switches/year if held across the full even-/odd-week block; do NOT re-trade intra-block). Enter the cyclical>defensive tilt on the first day of an even week, flip to defensive>cyclical on the first day of an odd week. Exit/flatten any leg only on the flip. XLY/XLP are penny-spread, so cost per flip ≈1-2bps/side on gross 1.0.
spec.instruments: XLY (consumer discretionary SPDR) long/short vs XLP (consumer staples SPDR) — both ultra-liquid S&P 500 sector ETFs already in the cache. Optionally generalize to a basket: long {XLY,XLK,XLF} / short {XLP,XLU,XLV} for more breadth and lower single-pair noise.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low — trades only liquid sector SPDRs (no single-name extreme deciles, no delisting/HTB exposure), so essentially immune to survivorship bias. The beta-neutral symmetric construction also avoids the net-long-cyclical tilt that would otherwise leak market beta (the directional even-only variant had ρ≈0.36-0.47 to SPY and is rejected for that reason).
spec.dailyFeedback: Each day log: cycle week index, isEven flag, the XLY-XLP spread return, cumulative sleeve PnL, realized net beta (trailing 60d regression on SPY), and trailing-60d/250d sleeve Sharpe. Daily even-vs-odd mean-spread monitor: if the even-minus-odd cyclical-defensive spread's trailing-250d t-stat drops below ~1, flag decay.
spec.falsifier: On strictly post-2021 OOS data, regress the daily sleeve return on a constant and SPY: REFUTED if (a) the intercept (net-of-beta alpha) is <=0 with t<1, OR (b) the even-week mean XLY-XLP spread is not statistically greater than the odd-week mean (one-sided, p>0.10), OR (c) net-of-cost OOS Sharpe at a realistic 2bps/side <= 0.10. NOTE: at a pessimistic 5bps/side the sleeve already nets ~0, so it ONLY clears the bar at the true ultra-liquid-ETF spread — that fragility is itself a standing falsifier to watch.
spec._family: Calendar/flow effects with a documented mechanism (FOMC information cycle + turn-of-month liquidity flow), constructed market-neutral and judged ruthlessly on post-2021 OOS net-of-cost Sharpe
