scores.name: Cross-Asset Trend L/S (multi-horizon, vol-targeted, market-neutral)
scores.oneLineRationale: Independently reproduced on the repo's own engine/cache: OOS net-beta Sharpe -1.32 (spec claimed +0.42), 0/24 configs clear the spec's own 0.15 falsifier, sign-shuffled placebo beats the real signal, LOCO makes it worse, and rho to the long-only sleeve spikes to +0.78 in the one crisis it claims to hedge — a known-rejected dollar-neutral class whose de-meaning step manufactures the negative edge; mean-orthogonal but tail-correlated noise that costs ~150bps borrow and 1028%/yr turnover to lose money.

spec.name: Cross-Asset Trend L/S (multi-horizon, vol-targeted, market-neutral)
spec.family: Time-series momentum / managed futures on a BROAD multi-asset ETF set, vol-scaled
spec.mechanism: Time-series momentum risk/behavioral premium (Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen 2012; Hurst-Ooi-Pedersen 'A Century of Evidence'). Slow under-reaction to fundamental shifts + initial-underreaction/delayed-overreaction means past trend predicts future return across asset classes. The EXISTING cross_asset_trend captures only the LONG side, leaving a residual net-long beta (its rho 0.11 to the book). Adding the SHORT leg on downtrending assets (a) neutralizes that residual beta, (b) harvests the documented short-side trend premium (trend works symmetrically — short bonds in 2022, short oil in 2014/2020), and (c) sharpens the crisis-alpha skew: into an equity selloff this sleeve is simultaneously LONG the assets trending up (TLT/GLD/UUP) AND SHORT the assets trending down (EEM/EFA/USO/HYG-via-credit), so it pays exactly when the long-equity/short-vol book bleeds. Multi-horizon (21/63/252-day) signal averaging is the literature's standard antidote to the post-2010 'no-trend/choppy' decay — it diversifies across the holding-period at which the trend pays off and halves single-window overfit.
spec.exactSignal: Universe = {TLT, IEF, SHY, GLD, SLV, DBC, USO, UUP, VNQ, EEM, EFA, LQD, HYG} (all in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS). For each asset compute three trend scores from adjusted closes: s1 = sign(totalReturn(c,21)), s3 = sign(totalReturn(c,63)), s12 = sign(momentumSkip(c,252,21)). Composite trend t = (s1+s3+s12)/3 in {-1,-1/3,1/3,1}. Inverse-vol weight w_raw = t / max(0.04, annualizedVol(c,60)). Drop assets with |t|<1/3 (no 2-of-3 horizon agreement) to cut whipsaw. Scale the whole vector so gross exposure sum(|w|)=1.0, then DOLLAR-NEUTRALIZE by subtracting the mean weight (sum(w)=0) and re-normalize gross to 1.0 — this strips the directional/beta tilt the existing sleeve keeps. Optional: cap any single name at 25% gross. Vol-target the realized sleeve to 10% ann (engine already vol-targets the book at 0.10).
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 21 trading days (monthly). Enter/flip a leg when 2-of-3 horizons agree on sign; exit to flat on that name when agreement drops below 2-of-3. No intramonth trading (positions drift between rebalances), matching the engine's drift model.
spec.instruments: 13 multi-asset ETFs: TLT, IEF, SHY (rates/curve), GLD, SLV (metals), DBC, USO (commodity/oil), UUP (dollar), VNQ (REIT), EEM, EFA (intl equity), LQD, HYG (credit). Signed weights; HYG/EEM short legs incur ~150bps borrow when shorted.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: Very low. ETFs only (no single-name selection), no extreme-decile equity bets, dollar-neutral across asset classes. None of these 13 ETFs has been delisted; no survivorship channel. The only data caveat is SLV/USO/UUP roll/contango drag, which is already IN the adjusted-close OHLCV so the backtest captures it honestly.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily runner recomputes the three horizon signs per asset and logs the composite t, the gross/net exposure (net should sit near 0), and per-leg contribution. Daily monitor: realized sleeve vol vs 10% target, and rolling 60d correlation of the sleeve return to (i) SPY and (ii) the existing cross_asset_trend return — alert if either exceeds 0.3 (signals the short leg has stopped neutralizing).
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data (2022-01 to today), the dollar-neutral L/S sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe is <= 0.15, OR its realized correlation to the existing long-only cross_asset_trend exceeds 0.5 (meaning the short leg adds no orthogonal information and it is just a levered copy). Either result refutes the 'short leg neutralizes residual beta and adds premium' thesis and the sleeve should be dropped.
spec._family: Time-series momentum / managed futures on a BROAD multi-asset ETF set, vol-scaled

scored.name: Sector-SPDR Lead-Lag Catch-Up (members chase their sector)
scored.family: Novel uncorrelated streams (sector lead-lag spillover, intra-market realized-vol / overnight-intraday dispersion)

scores.name: Sector-SPDR Lead-Lag Catch-Up (members chase their sector)
scores.oneLineRationale: Reproduced the exact-spec backtest myself: OOS post-2021 net Sharpe -0.69 (net-of-beta -0.88), IC ~0.011 with 50.5% positive (coin flip), IS gross 1.13 collapsing to OOS 0.34 with the parameter map inverting (gate 1.5% OOS 0.34 vs 3.0% OOS 0.98) — classic curve-fit; the g=r_sec-r_stock signal is a 3-5d reversal bet (a confirmed repo null) mis-labeled as Hou-2007 monthly portfolio lead-lag and applied to mega-caps that dominate their own ETF, so the premise is inverted; even zero-friction OOS is ~flat so it is not a cost problem but no-edge, while 13,130%/yr turnover + 150bps HTB borrow and rhoSPY 0.21 with a pro-cyclical crash tail mean the low corr-to-book is real but worthless on a negative stream — all four red-team lenses Fatal, trips its own falsifier, CUT.

spec.name: Sector-SPDR Lead-Lag Catch-Up (members chase their sector)
spec.mechanism: Documented: intra-industry slow information diffusion (Hou 2007 RFS; Cohen-Lou; 'frog in the pan' JFE 2021). The sector SPDR (a liquid, instantly-priced basket) impounds sector-wide news FASTER than its slower individual member stocks. A member whose own 1-5d return has LAGGED its sector ETF's move tends to catch up over the next few days. This is a structural latency in price discovery (analyst/flow attention is concentrated on the index/ETF), not a curve-fit; it is documented to persist (does not reverse in months 2-12).
spec.exactSignal: For each of the 11 sector SPDRs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLB/XLU/XLC/XLRE), map its EQUITY_UNIVERSE members (assign each of the ~165 names to its SPDR via a static GICS sector map — to be added; sector ETFs themselves are never survivorship-biased). For each member s compute the 5-day return r_s(5) and its sector ETF's 5-day return r_sec(5). Compute the lead-lag GAP g_s = r_sec(5) - r_s(5) (how far the stock lags its sector). Within EACH sector, z-score g_s across that sector's members (sector-neutral). LONG members with the highest positive gap (sector rose, stock hasn't followed); SHORT members with the most negative gap (sector fell, stock hasn't dropped). Require |r_sec(5)| > 1.5%% (only act when the sector actually moved — a real signal, not noise). Equal-weight within sign, dollar-neutral (gross ~1.0), and equalize long vs short notional WITHIN each sector so the net sector beta is ~0.
spec.entryExit: Rebalance every 3 trading days (rebalanceDays:3). Enter the cross-sectional long/short gap portfolio; a name exits when its gap z-score reverts toward 0 (it caught up) or flips sign. Hold close-to-close. warmupBars ~210 (need 200d for the static universe filter + 5d windows). Cap any single name at 1/ (members in sector) so no concentration.
spec.instruments: ~150 EQUITY_UNIVERSE single names grouped under their sector SPDR; sector SPDRs read for the leader signal only (not traded). Signed weights via the existing L/S engine.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: LOW-MEDIUM: built sector-neutral within SPDR member groups, not extreme-decile single-name bets, and the leader (sector ETF) is itself survivorship-free. Dead/delisted names only enter as small sector-relative legs that net against live peers; their absence biases far less than a raw bottom-decile short. The static GICS map must be point-in-time-safe (assign sector by listing, not by today's membership).
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: log realized 3-day forward return of the gap portfolio vs the sector-neutral benchmark; track the cross-sectional rank correlation between today's gap z-score and the next-3d member return (the signal's IC). A 60-day rolling IC that decays toward 0 (or flips negative) flags decay before P&L does. Also monitor turnover and borrow on the short legs.
spec.falsifier: On post-2021 OOS data: if the 60-day rolling information coefficient between gap z-score and next-3d member excess return is <= 0 for a majority of months, OR the net-of-beta after-cost OOS (post-2021) Sharpe is < 0.2, OR the strategy's net sector beta drifts above 0.15, the sleeve is refuted and dropped. (Costs: ~5bps/side + 150bps borrow on shorts; ~3-day turnover keeps cost drag manageable but it is the binding risk.)
spec._family: Novel uncorrelated streams (sector lead-lag spillover, intra-market realized-vol / overnight-intraday dispersion)

scored.family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S

scores.name: 52-Week-High Proximity Momentum (George-Hwang), sector-neutral L/S
scores.oneLineRationale: Repro (scripts/backtest/_redteam_52wh.mjs, confirmed by re-run) shows the L/S stream is hidden 12-1 momentum (realized rho 0.59 full / 0.67 OOS / 0.88 in 2022, tripping its own 0.55 falsifier) and OOS-negative (-0.33 after cost, -0.19 gross before cost), so the orthogonality premise is empirically false; the short leg is survivorship-inflated (SIVB/FRC absent, COIN/RIVN/ARM backfilled) with too-low 150bps borrow, the sector map is unimplemented in the book, and resid_momentum (rho ~-0.13 to SPY) already owns a strictly better neutralized-momentum sleeve.

spec.name: 52-Week-High Proximity Momentum (George-Hwang), sector-neutral L/S
spec.family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S
spec.mechanism: Behavioral anchoring (George-Hwang 2004, 'The 52-Week High and Momentum Investing'): traders anchor to the salient 52-week-high price and under-react when a stock approaches/breaks it, so nearness-to-52w-high predicts continued outperformance. Crucially the signal is a price LEVEL (close / 52w-high), not a trailing RETURN, so it is mechanically PARTLY DISTINCT from the 12-1 trailing-return momentum already in the book (documented ρ to Jegadeesh-Titman momentum ~0.4-0.6) and from resid_momentum. It is therefore the least-correlated GENUINE-momentum horizon to add: it captures momentum information the 12-1 leg misses (a stock can be near its 52w high without a large trailing 12-1 return, and vice-versa). Sector-neutralizing cuts the concentration into whichever sector is trending — the survivorship/concentration reduction the brief asks for.
spec.exactSignal: For each equity s: prox(s) = closes[last] / max(closes over last 252 bars). Build the same static sector map as Sleeve 1. Sector-neutralize: score(s) = prox(s) - mean(prox over s's sector members) (intra-sector nearness-to-high). Rank cross-sectionally: LONG top-quintile by score (nearest their 52w high within their sector), SHORT bottom-quintile (furthest below their sector's highs), equal-weight, dollar-neutral (0.5/0.5). Beta-neutralize the residual exposure by sizing each leg's net beta to ~0 using beta() vs SPY (scale the smaller-beta leg up modestly so portfolio beta ~0).
spec.entryExit: Monthly rebalance (rebalanceDays=21) — low turnover, matches the slow-moving level signal and keeps costs minimal. Enter equal-weight quintile L/S on sector-neutral 52w-high proximity; exit on monthly refresh as names enter/leave the extreme quintiles. Inputs through i-1 (no-look-ahead).
spec.instruments: ~165 large/mid-cap US equities (EQUITY_UNIVERSE); SPY for beta-neutralization. Signed long+short weights; 150bps borrow on the short quintile.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: LOW: sector-demeaned quintile L/S on a price-LEVEL ratio (close/52w-high is bounded in (0,1] and far less extreme than a return-decile), so it does not concentrate in blown-up survivors; the short leg (far below sector highs) is the only mild survivorship channel. Lower turnover (monthly) further reduces sensitivity.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: rolling 60d ρ of this sleeve's returns to xs_momentum and resid_momentum (this is the load-bearing metric — if ρ creeps above ~0.5 the sleeve is redundant and should be cut). Monthly: net-of-cost alpha and net portfolio beta (confirm |beta| < 0.1).
spec.falsifier: On post-2021-06 OOS data, the sector+beta-neutral monthly 52w-high L/S has OOS net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2 after costs, OR its realized ρ to the existing 12-1/resid momentum book is >= +0.55 (i.e. it is just relabeled 12-1 momentum and adds no diversification). Either refutes it.
spec._family: Multi-horizon cross-sectional momentum (1/3/6/12m), beta- & sector-neutral L/S

scores.name: Volume-Confirmed Residual Reversal L/S (high-MAX / capitulation-conditioned, weekly)
scores.oneLineRationale: Base family is an EXECUTED NULL in this exact harness (st_reversal Sharpe 0.22, net-of-beta -0.55, 31,401%/yr turnover, ratio(SPY) 0.67 — "dead, confirmed twice"); the volume/capitulation gate must conjure a ~0.9-Sharpe swing from pre-cost, triple-conditioned, many-trials literature (verified results.md:26,64). Claimed ρ=0 is a full-sample artifact masking crisis-conditional ρ ~+0.4-0.7 to the momentum/short-vol book via gross-up-in-stress hidden beta, a shared high-MAX crash tail, and the fact that long-losers/short-winners IS anti-momentum (a re-skin of the shipped ltreversal sleeve). Survivorship is structural: EQUITY_UNIVERSE (universe.ts) is a static 2026 survivor list explicitly stocked with the exact high-MAX/HTB cohort it tilts into (COIN/HOOD/AFRM/SOFI/MSTR/RIVN/CVNA/SMCI), so delisted blowups are absent and both legs are inflated; costs.ts charges a flat 150bps borrow while its own comment concedes the real cohort runs 100-1000bps. Honest OOS net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.07 (lens medians 0.15/0.08/0.00/0.05), it trips its own falsifiers (b standalone>0.2 and c |ρ|>0.25), and the simpler, lower-turnover Residual-Momentum L/S incumbent (residmom.ts, +0.43 net-of-beta, ρ(SPY) -0.13) already dominates it — CUT per Occam.

spec.name: Volume-Confirmed Residual Reversal L/S (high-MAX / capitulation-conditioned, weekly)
spec.family: Short-term reversal as MARKET-NEUTRAL L/S (long 1-5d losers, short winners), cost-aware
spec.mechanism: Same liquidity-provision/overreaction premium, but CONDITIONED on the documented amplifier: reversal is strongest precisely after high-attention, high-volume overreaction (NY Fed SR-513 decomposition; 'Maxing out short-term reversals' 2025 shows high-MAX lottery names exhibit the largest, most reliable reversals because retail overreaction is biggest there). Requiring an abnormal-volume + large-residual-move filter selects the subset of names where the bounce is economically real, which raises per-trade edge and lets the sleeve trade FEWER names (lower turnover, lower borrow drag) than an unconditional reversal book.
spec.exactSignal: Start from Spec 1's residual 5d score on the same liquidity-screened EQUITY_UNIVERSE. Add a volume-confirmation gate: only ELIGIBLE if the most recent move was on abnormal volume — volume[t]/SMA(volume,20) > 1.5 AND the single-day residual return on day t (r1_stock - b*r1_SPY) is in the top/bottom 10% of the name's own trailing 60-day residual-return distribution (a capitulation/blowoff z-event, |z|>1.5). Among eligible names: long the residual losers (negative residual blowoff on heavy volume), short the residual winners (positive residual blowoff on heavy volume). Equal-weight, dollar-neutral, cap at ~12 longs + 12 shorts (concentrate on the strongest signals). If fewer than 6 per side are eligible, scale gross down proportionally (sit partly in cash) rather than reaching for weak signals.
spec.entryExit: Weekly rebalance (5d). Enter only names passing BOTH the residual-extreme AND abnormal-volume gates at the rebalance close; long residual-loser capitulations, short residual-winner blowoffs. Hold 5 days, exit at next rebalance. Gross floats 0.3-1.0 depending on how many capitulation events exist that week (signal-gated exposure — naturally heavier after volatile, high-overreaction weeks, consistent with the premium being a volatility/liquidity-stress compensation).
spec.instruments: Long & short the EQUITY_UNIVERSE large/mid-caps (signed weights); SPY for beta/residual only. Volume from the OHLCV Bar (volume field) is fully available. Engine charges 6bps round-trip + 150bps borrow.
spec.survivorshipSensitivity: LOW-MEDIUM. The volume/capitulation gate trades fewer names and is event-driven rather than always-on, so it is less exposed to the static survivor list than a full-decile book; but conditioning on high-MAX/blowoff names tilts slightly toward higher-vol tickers, partially reoffsetting the residualization benefit — net roughly the same as Spec 1. The signal-gated cash buffer (scaling down when few events) reduces forced exposure to thin tails.
spec.dailyFeedback: Daily: log the number of capitulation events triggered, realized 1-5d bounce per event vs the historical conditional mean, and current gross exposure. Track whether high-volume-confirmed reversals still outperform the unconditional Spec-1 book on a 60-day rolling basis (the conditioning must EARN its extra complexity). Alert if event count collapses to ~0 for a month (regime where overreaction is muted) or if conditional edge < unconditional edge for 8+ weeks (drop the gate).
spec.falsifier: Refuted on post-2021 OOS if (a) the volume-conditioned book's net-of-beta Sharpe does NOT exceed the unconditional Spec-1 book by at least +0.1 (i.e., the conditioning adds no value and should be discarded), OR (b) its standalone net-of-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is <= 0.2, OR (c) realized correlation to the existing momentum-heavy book exceeds +0.25 in absolute value. If the gate fails to beat the simpler sleeve, prefer Spec 1 (Occam / anti-overfit).

panel.lens: Max-Sharpe PM — ruthless overfitting filter. A new sleeve helps ONLY if its honest OOS net-of-beta Sharpe is POSITIVE after realistic costs AND its true ρ to the existing book is low. Low ρ to a money-losing stream is dilution, not diversification. I judge every candidate on its post-2021 OOS number and its TRUE (not claimed) correlation/beta, not its in-sample headline. Leverage is irrelevant (Sharpe is scale-invariant). The book's honest OOS baseline is 0.91 and already bakes in all internal cross-sleeve diversification, so I add sleeves to 0.91 (no double-counting).
selectedSleeves: Industry Long-Term Reversal — Sector-SPDR L/S (36-12m), shipped in its FIXED form: net-of-beta low-correlation defensive ballast, |rho|<0.35 & |beta|<0.25 falsifier (NOT the spec's false strict-negativity claim), restricted to the clean all-11-SPDR window, 12-1 momentum window excluded from the sign-flip test, sized TINY
panel.whatItWouldTake: Reaching 2.0 from the 0.91 book requires the new sleeves to add sum-of-squared-Sharpe ≈ 3.17 (since 0.91² + Σsᵢ² = 4.0). That is ~9 genuinely-uncorrelated new sleeves at s̄≈0.6, or ~13 at s̄≈0.5, all at ρ̄≈0 OOS after costs. The entire scored search surfaced exactly ONE positive-OOS low-ρ sleeve at ~0.3-0.5 — contributing ~0.13 to Σsᵢ². The remaining ~3.0 of required squared-Sharpe does not exist in this opportunity set (free daily OHLCV, ≤1 leverage, no real options chains, 100%-survivor mega/large-cap cross-section). To actually get there you would need a structurally different opportunity set: (a) point-in-time / survivorship-free data (CRSP/Norgate) to make extreme-decile single-name L/S honest and widen the cross-section to ~500-3000 names so dispersion-driven sleeves (residual reversal, IVOL, BAB) stop inverting; (b) genuine option convexity via real historical IV surfaces to harvest the VRP that cannot be backtested here; (c) intraday open→close execution to capture the overnight-vs-intraday tug-of-war that the EOD engine books with the wrong sign. None are in scope. Honest ceiling here is ~1.0-1.1, not 2.0.
panel.whyUncorrelated: The one selected sleeve is a sector-SPDR L/S whose net-of-beta stream has stable β≈-0.117 and realized |ρ| to the book near zero (+0.01/-0.10 to sector_rotation, not the spec's claimed strict negativity). Its mechanism — long-term (36-12m) reversal as a price-based VALUE proxy — is structurally orthogonal to the book's MOMENTUM core (value and momentum are the classic negatively-related styles), so it decorrelates without sharing the book's long-equity/trend-following beta. Critically, it is NOT one of the false-orthogonality traps the rest of the list fell into: it does not smuggle in residual long-SPY beta (style/coint pairs left +0.30-0.63 hidden beta), it is not collinear with low_vol (BAB-sectors true ρ 0.30-0.40), and it does not share a short-gamma left tail that would CONCENTRATE crash risk on the put book (its selloff tail is favorable). It runs on the 11 liquid SPDRs, so it is far less survivorship-sensitive than raw extreme-decile single-name bets.
panel.overfittingRisk: HIGH for the set as a whole, which is exactly why it is one sleeve, not eight. The just-completed audit already showed the prior 1.14 was inflated by look-ahead (→0.87), survivorship, and in-sample tuning, so the prior is that apparent edges evaporate OOS. Concretely: (1) 14 of 17 scored candidates trip their OWN falsifiers OOS and several invert sign on leave-2022-out — the canonical overfit signature; their low ρ is real but their alpha is in-sample noise. (2) Even the survivor is fragile: its tradable raw signal collapses 0.30→0.04 leave-2022-out (a value-vs-growth bet concentrated in one cycle, ~3 cycles total), and only its NET-OF-BETA residual (~0.52 alpha-Sharpe, β -0.117) survives — so I ship only the beta-stripped ballast version, sized tiny, with a hard |ρ|<0.35 & |β|<0.25 daily tripwire that auto-benches it. Guards: judge OOS-only, size by realized rolling performance (self-tracking floor starves decay), never fund an orthogonal-but-negative stream, and require a mechanism that is value/momentum-grounded rather than a band/threshold curve-fit. Expected portfolio Sharpe is therefore quoted at 0.96 (book 0.91 + sleeve at its honest TRADABLE 0.27, ρ0), NOT the 1.05 the optimistic 0.52 alpha-Sharpe would give — I deliberately do not bank the optimistic number.
panel.biggestRisk: The 2022-style stocks-AND-bonds-down regime (the audit's R2): the book collapses to its equity beta precisely when its diversifiers (cross-asset-trend bond legs + TLT tail hedge) break together, and the one new sleeve's honest edge is itself concentrated in 2022 — so the diversifier I am adding is correlated to the book's single worst regime rather than orthogonal to it, the opposite of what I need. Compounding this is survivorship bias: the 100%-survivor universe both flatters the existing momentum/residual-momentum alpha (true OOS likely below the measured 0.91) AND structurally inverts the very dispersion sleeves (residual reversal, IVOL, BAB) that would otherwise have been the additional uncorrelated streams needed to approach 2.0. The result is a hard, data-grounded ceiling around 1.0: the honest, defensible deliverable is a ~0.95-1.0 Sharpe book at roughly one-third of SPY's drawdown (the real edge is drawdown control, not high Sharpe), and claiming 2.0 from this opportunity set would be marketing, not measurement.

panel.lens: Overfitting skeptic — deflated-Sharpe / OOS-robustness lens. I judge every candidate on its post-2021 net-of-beta Sharpe AFTER realistic costs, on parameter-stability (does the sign survive a horizon/leg-count sweep), on cycle count (how many independent crises actually drive the result), and on the multiple-testing deflation implied by the ~17 specs that were tried. A sleeve only counts if it is decades-robust in the literature, low-parameter, and its edge does not evaporate when the single dominant regime (2022 value-vs-growth) is removed. Fewer, more robust sleeves beat a larger fragile basket. I reproduced the surviving candidate on the repo's own 11y cache (scripts/backtest/_redteam_ltrev_sector.mjs) rather than trusting the scorecard.
selectedSleeves: Industry Long-Term Reversal — Sector-SPDR L/S (36-12m), de-rated and rebranded as a low-correlation defensive ballast, sized tiny, with falsifier rewritten to |rho|<0.35 & |beta|<0.25
panel.whatItWouldTake: Reaching 2.0 honestly is out of reach in this mandate; within it, the realistic program is: stack the ONE surviving ballast plus any future sleeves that clear a pre-registered OOS gate, target an honest ceiling of ~1.0-1.3, and unlock the higher ceiling only by widening the data (survivorship-free 1000+ name universe, real option chains, or intraday execution) — each a deliberate infra decision, not a backtest tweak.
panel.whyUncorrelated: The single selected sleeve is genuinely low-rho to the existing momentum-heavy book for a documented mechanism: 36-12m long-term reversal is the price-based VALUE proxy (DeBondt-Thaler 1985; Fama-French long-term reversal factor), and value is the canonical negative-correlation diversifier to a momentum/trend book (the value-vs-momentum negative correlation is one of the most-replicated cross-sectional facts). I reproduced it on the cache: stable beta -0.117, rho to sector_rotation +0.02/+0.03 (near-zero), favorable selloff tail. Critically I am NOT claiming the spec's structurally-negative correlation — that claim is empirically false (realized rho is ~0, not negative) and self-refuting; I rebrand it honestly as near-zero-rho ballast. It is the ONLY survivor precisely because most 'market-neutral' candidates here carry hidden correlation: BAB-Sectors and IVOL-neutral are really long-XLU/XLP/XLV short-growth bets collinear with the funded low_vol sleeve (true rho 0.30-0.44), and the sector-neutral LT-residual-reversal has rho -0.34 to -0.65 to resid_momentum (a disguised SHORT of the book's best survivor). Orthogonality on this thin 11-ETF/165-name 100%-survivor cross-section is far scarcer than the scorecard's corrToBook column suggests.
panel.overfittingRisk: HIGH and explicitly named. The DEFLATED-SHARPE risk: ~17 specs were tried on one 11y sample, so the highest observed Sharpe is upward-biased by multiple testing (Bailey-Lopez de Prado deflated Sharpe). The repo already lived this — the prior 1.14 headline deflated to 0.87 (look-ahead bug) then 0.91 OOS. For the surviving sleeve specifically: (a) horizon sign-instability — my sweep gives 12-1=-0.09, 18-1=-0.25, 30-6=+0.53, 36-12=+0.26 net-of-beta; the sign flips across adjacent windows, the hallmark of curve-fit, so the 36-12 choice is itself a selected parameter; (b) regime concentration — the OOS net-of-beta 0.62 is largely the single 2022 value-vs-growth episode; leave-2022-out collapses the raw signal to ~0.04 and the alpha-stream to ~0.52, and there are only ~3 independent cycles in 11y; (c) survivorship — even the SPDR construction is on a fixed-membership ETF set. GUARDS: size it TINY (it is ballast, not a return engine); rewrite the ship-gate to |rho|<0.35 & |beta|<0.25 rather than the false strict-negativity test; LOCK the 36-12 horizon and the 4x4 legs before any live data (no re-tuning); exclude the 12-1 momentum window from the sign-test; restrict to the clean all-11-SPDR window; run an honest leave-one-crisis-out and a 2025+ OOS falsifier that auto-benches if post-deploy net-of-beta Sharpe goes <=0 over >=40 obs; and apply a deflated-Sharpe haircut to its prior in the allocator so the self-tracking loop does not over-fund it on the 2022-flattered number.
panel.biggestRisk: The biggest risk is FALSE PRECISION / diversification theater: shipping a basket of 'market-neutral, low-rho' sleeves whose backtested orthogonality is an artifact of a too-thin, 100%-survivor cross-section, and whose tiny positive expectancy inverts out-of-sample. The candidate evidence is brutal and consistent: of 17 specs, 16 have OOS net-of-beta Sharpe <= 0, and several that look orthogonal (corrToBook ~0) are 'orthogonality-without-alpha' — uncorrelated streams that reliably LOSE money, which is dilution, not diversification, and in some cases (short-gamma reversal/pairs legs) actively CONCENTRATE crash risk on the existing put book (tail-orthogonality != mean-orthogonality). Adding any of the CUT sleeves to chase N would LOWER the honest portfolio Sharpe. The disciplined move is to add the one real ballast small, keep the book at an honest ~0.95-1.0, and refuse to manufacture 2.0 — the number the repo would be tempted to market is exactly the number that already blew up once (1.14 -> 0.91).

panel.whatItWouldTake: The identity s_bar*sqrt(N/(1+(N-1)*rho_bar)) requires roughly 8-12 GENUINELY uncorrelated sleeves at s_bar~=0.6-0.8 OOS net-of-beta with rho_bar~=0. This free-daily-EOD-OHLCV, leverage<=1 universe yields at most ONE marginal new sleeve at s~=0.27-0.30 (the SPDR LT-reversal ballast); every other scored candidate is CUT (negative/sign-inverted OOS or orthogonal-but-empty). To actually reach 2.0 you must add NEW data/edge classes that this environment forbids: (1) the real options IV surface + skew (the live put-VRP edge — already BUILD-for-LIVE, ASSERT-only for backtest, no free history); (2) intraday open->close execution to capture the overnight-vs-intraday and 1-5d residual-reversal effects that DO exist in the literature but invert/vanish on the close-to-close EOD engine; (3) a much wider single-name cross-section (1000s of names) to restore the BAB/IVOL dispersion that collapses to noise on 11 SPDRs or 165 survivors; (4) genuine convexity (defined-risk options) — also blocked. Without one of these, the honest ceiling is ~1.0, not 2.0 — exactly what the prior 141-agent fleet and the audit independently concluded.
panel.whyUncorrelated: The single shippable addition is uncorrelated to the book for a real STRUCTURAL reason, not a fitted one: it is a 36-12m mean-reversion (De Bondt-Thaler long-horizon reversal, a price-based VALUE proxy) on SECTOR aggregates, while the book is momentum-heavy (xs_momentum + factor_momentum + resid_momentum all 12-1 continuation). Value and momentum are the canonical negatively-correlated pair (Asness-Moskowitz-Pedersen). Measured realized rho-to-book is +0.01/-0.10 (near-zero) with stable beta -0.117 — so it adds a mildly defensive, low-rho stream. CAUTION: its claimed STRICT-negative correlation is empirically FALSE (near-zero, not negative); ship it only on the honest |rho|<0.35 framing, never as a guaranteed hedge. The KEEP sleeves (resid_momentum, lt_reversal) are dollar+beta-neutral single-name L/S whose returns live in idiosyncratic dispersion orthogonal to the long-only beta book (rho -0.13, -0.12).
panel.overfittingRisk: HIGH and explicitly guarded. The whole point of this exercise is that the prior 1.14 was inflated by a look-ahead bug (->0.87), survivorship, and in-sample tuning. Guards: (1) ship ONLY sleeves with positive post-2021 OOS net-of-beta Sharpe AFTER costs, computed by the same no-look-ahead engine that produced the 0.96 baseline; (2) the one survivor must pass leave-2022-out (alpha-Sharpe ~0.52 survives) — its raw signal is a fragile single-cycle 2022 value-vs-growth bet (only ~3 cycles, horizon sign-unstable), so it ships SIZED TINY (gross<=0.10) and rebranded as ballast, never as a Sharpe engine; (3) hard-coded falsifier |rho|<0.35 & |beta|<0.25 with a daily tripwire that zero-weights it if breached; (4) prefer the 11-SPDR (survivorship-light) construction over extreme single-name deciles whose short legs (NVDA/AMD/SMCI/COIN) caused the BAB/IVOL candidates to invert in this 100%-survivor universe; (5) refuse to fund ANY uncorrelated-but-negative-EV stream — orthogonality to a losing stream is dilution. Net: assume near-zero genuine new alpha and size accordingly.
panel.biggestRisk: The decisive risk is that the menu contains no path to 2.0 and the temptation is to ship CUT sleeves anyway because they are 'uncorrelated' (corrToBook ~0). That is the exact overfitting trap: 14 of 16 candidates have oosSharpe <=0.08 (most negative, several sign-inverted vs their thesis), so funding them ADDS NEGATIVE EV while looking diversifying — a near-zero-rho stream with negative OOS expected return drags the book down, it does not lift Sharpe. Secondary risk: even the one survivor's economic thesis (structurally-negative hedge) is self-refuting (realized rho near zero, not negative; its own daily-rho alert auto-fires ~51% of windows), so mis-sizing it as a hedge would leave a hidden ~-0.12 beta and ~3 effective cycles of evidence — it must be a tiny ballast, not a load-bearing diversifier. The honest deliverable is: ship the 1 survivor tiny, keep the existing 2 L/S diversifiers, report ~1.0 truthfully, and state plainly that 2.0 is unreachable without options-surface / intraday / wider-cross-section data this environment does not provide.

Reaching 2.0 needs a structurally different opportunity set, each an infra decision not a backtest tweak: survivorship-free point-in-time data (CRSP/Norgate) plus about 500-3000 names so dispersion sleeves (residual reversal, IVOL, BAB) stop inverting (here their short legs ARE the decade winners NVDA/AMD/SMCI/COIN); real historical option IV surface and skew for the put-VRP that cannot be backtested here; and intraday open-to-close execution for the overnight-vs-intraday and 1-5d reversal that the close-to-close engine books with the wrong sign. None in mandate. Honest ceiling about 1.0-1.1; the real edge is drawdown control (about one-third of SPY drawdown), not a high Sharpe.
assembly.combinationNote: Add to the 0.91 baseline, not the 0.96/1.14 headline, with no double-counting. The new sleeve enters at its honest tradable 0.30 (rho near 0): 0.91 to about 0.97 via sqrt of (0.91 squared plus 0.30 squared). Do not bank the optimistic 0.52 net-of-beta (which would imply about 1.05) because that alpha is concentrated in the single 2022 cycle (raw collapses 0.30 to 0.04 leave-2022-out; about 3 cycles total) and the horizon sign is unstable (verified: 12-1 is -0.08, 18-1 is -0.25, 30-6 is +0.53, 36-12 is +0.26), so 36-12 is itself a selected parameter. Apply a deflated-Sharpe haircut (Bailey-Lopez de Prado; 17 specs on one sample inflate the max) to the allocator prior. Critical: its honest edge is concentrated in 2022, the book worst regime (stocks-and-bonds-down), so it is near-zero-rho ballast, not a hedge against the actual worst drawdown. Ship tiny; let the realized-rolling floor starve it on decay.
assembly.oosProtocol: Pre-registered, OOS-only gate for this and any future candidate. One, post-2021 net-of-beta Sharpe after costs (10bps round-trip plus about 50bps borrow, 150bps if HTB) through the same no-look-ahead engine that produced the 0.91/0.96 baseline. Two, require positive OOS net-of-beta after costs; an orthogonal-but-negative stream is auto-rejected. Three, survive leave-2022-out; the alpha-stream survives about 0.52 but the raw signal does not, so only the beta-stripped version ships. Four, the sign must not flip across adjacent horizon and leg-count windows before deploy; lock 36-12 and 4x4 with no re-tuning. Five, measure true rho and beta on realized daily returns; ship-gate is abs-rho under 0.35 and abs-beta under 0.25 with a daily tripwire that auto-benches if breached. Six, live 2025-plus falsifier auto-benches if post-deploy net-of-beta turns non-positive over at least 40 observations. Seven, apply a deflated-Sharpe haircut to the sleeve allocator prior. Eight, prefer survivorship-light SPDR construction over single-name deciles. Size by realized rolling performance.
assembly.rejected: Rejected all 16 other candidates; funding any would lower the honest Sharpe. Orthogonal-but-negative-EV (dilution): Turn-of-Month (OOS -0.21, inverted), Short-Term Residual Reversal 1wk (OOS -0.75, about 7800pct turnover), Overnight-vs-Intraday (OOS -0.15, edge in the intraday leg the EOD engine cannot book), FOMC-Cycle (premium inverted in-sample). Hidden-beta or false-orthogonality: Style-Spread MR and Cointegrated Pairs (near-unit-root, plus 0.30 to 0.63 hidden beta, already CUT), Slope-Timed Duration Carry (on-state rho-to-TLT 0.93, plus 3.30 hidden duration-years, OOS -0.42), RV Cross-Asset Spread Trend (credit leg plus 0.20 SPY beta, true rho 0.18-0.32). Dispersion sleeves that invert on the thin survivor cross-section: BAB-Sectors (collinear with low_vol, rho 0.30-0.40, OOS 0.08), IVOL-neutral (OOS -0.40, short leg IS the winners), Defensive-Factor RS (strips the low-beta channel that pays). Short-gamma or tail-concentrating (orthogonal in mean, shares the put book left tail): VIX Term-Structure (OOS -0.34, long-VIXY bleeds -12.2pct/yr), Sector-Neutral LT Residual Reversal (rho -0.34 to -0.65 to resid_momentum, a disguised short of the best survivor; OOS near 0), Sector-SPDR RV Reversion (sign backwards, OOS -0.6), Residual 5-Day Reversal (sign backwards, OOS -0.5). Also rejected the survivor spec-as-written: its structurally-negative-mirror thesis is empirically false (realized rho plus 0.015/0.026, near-zero; daily rho-above-0 alert fires about 51pct), so it ships only as near-zero-rho ballast, never a hedge. And rejected quoting any number above about 1.0: that is the 1.14 headline that already deflated to 0.91 once.
